# Thread: CT Diminishing Returns
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-diminishing-returns.23793/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Roger Seagunn — 2019-06-05T04:25:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-diminishing-returns.23793/post-272042>

Question:
I've been doing CT on and off for about 2 years now. It used to be when I got out I would feel like I had several cups of coffee (very high energy). I don't anymore. I Still feel the anti-inflammatory effects, but not the super-burst of energy I would. Do you think that I'm simply adapted to the level of cold? Similar to how you can adapt to a level of exercise a nd not endorphin-rush? Should I simply go deeper and colder? or should I start eating more of something/supplement to get the rush back?

### kris90 — 2019-06-05T18:00:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-diminishing-returns.23793/post-272070>

It could be a sign of adaptation. I think you can only go so far with cold. If you need to raise your dopamine or get that adrenaline hit any higher, try other things. Do things that scare you or out of your ordinary. Take a different way home. Take a trip by yourself. Meet new people, etc.

The only other idea I have is if your environment changed. Always pay attention to changes occurring around you.

### shah78 — 2019-06-05T21:43:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-diminishing-returns.23793/post-272098>

What is your routine that has stopped working? I think you need to go colder and / or longer. Eat lots more fish for a week. Good luck

### Roger Seagunn — 2019-06-05T21:51:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-diminishing-returns.23793/post-272100>

The water is about 40-45 degrees. I usually do 20m in a tub.Interestingly, I went full-body swimming in a cold pool that was opening up for spring a few weeks ago...it must have been in the 50s and I felt the rush again. Does it matter if the HEAD is under the water for the 'feel-good' hormones to come out more easily? I guess when I go longer, I have harder recovery times...but I don't seem any more euphoric XD. I will try though to push it harder this week (30m-45m).

### Sean Waters — 2019-06-06T13:42:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-diminishing-returns.23793/post-272122>

[@Roger Seagunn](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/13742/) ...some days I do, some days I don't.

Same with the Sun too, strong AM Sun can give me that rush, goosebumps and amazing feeling, but generally it doesn't do much but make me feel chilled and content.

I'm not sure "chasing" euphoria is a wise path to take. But I have definitely chased before, several times... who wouldn't want to feel like that all the time?

But I think that's the deal - it wouldn't be productive or beneficial or probably that nice either... bit like Chocolate Cake... its good for a while, then you can start to have enough. Better in small, sporadic doses.

# Thread: Vermont 2019
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vermont-2019.23794/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 29
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2019-06-05T17:40:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vermont-2019.23794/post-272064>

Just thought I would share a summary of my experience this past weekend in Vermont.

I definitely missed Jack this year and all our misfits who did not attend Nourish Vermont 2019, but something drove me to go anyways. Shelburne Farms is a beautiful, peaceful place full of energy, and I needed a weekend getaway as I've been really trying to find myself within the past couple months.

All I can say was despite not having fellow mitochondriacs in my presence, I still had an incredible dopamine-boosting experience. There were some great speakers: Beth Lambert on our childhood health decline, Jason Prall regarding lifestyle & longevity, Nic Pineault on EMF exposure and Zach Bush on carbon cycles and our depleted soil. There was definitely a common theme and takeaway for all the talks and that was a sense of community and belonging is extremely important in health and happiness. Being lonely in this world has major implications and negative impact on our overall health and wellbeing. Humans have always needed connection and interaction and it likely has to do with how we sample each other's electrons and biophotons.

Another interesting note was that although Jack was not invited this year, his message about reconnecting to nature and living a naked outdoor life was clearly received and conveyed in each and every one of the speakers including Zach Bush. While Zach mentioned again the negative impact of glyphosate on the tight junctions within the intestinal tract, he stated that the best mitigation of this was to be naked outside, and even used Jeff Leech's findings on the Hadza to prove this point as Jack had last year in Vermont.

I've got to say, I really respect and love Zach Bush as a person. As a salesman, not so much, but there's so much more to him than that. The energy he brought force and shared with everyone (myself included) was nothing short of amazing. The way he can light up the room with his love and energy for people blew me away. I spent Friday evening with Zach 1-on-1, and immediately he could sense some of my pain and suffering for which he taught me an important lesson about being present and focusing on NOW. The past few years have been important in terms of my own healing and reversing my health ailments such as: hypogonadism, adrenal fatigue, seasonal depression and leaky gut, but I realised that once healing and reversal is complete, we must begin to come back to a grounded state. This never occurred for me, because I continue to live in fear that I may become ill again. Zach reassured me that this would not occur, it simply cannot. Once you reverse a disease, you have that accomplishment under your belt and you simply know how to overcome it, and so it would be impossible to go back to that state. He could sense my sympathetic dominance and lack of activation of my parasympathetic nervous system and could feel my intensity. He taught me to become more like a flower. Just be. Just live. If it's raining out, be wet and cold. If the sun is shining, be dry and warm. Go with the flow of life, and live in the moment. I really needed this message. Energy comes from anywhere in the universe. My obsessed behavior in remaining healthy the past few years has created such intensity and caused me to vibrate at a difference wavelength than the majority of the world and I've pushed some people I love dearly away. I feel myself falling back down to the ground state like an excited electron, and giving off my excess energy to those I love. I want to light up a room and attract people toward me and bring them up to a higher level, as well as bring myself up. This is my new mission, my priority, my purpose. When I received this message from Zach, I immediately had a desire to receive a hug from him to sample his energy. I have to say, it was the most powerful, healing hug I have ever receive in my life. From that moment, I remained present the rest of my trip, and Zach commented on the dramatic transformation that had taken place instantaneously by admiring what he called my "beautiful smile" and "beautiful person" that I am. Not once did Zach EVER try to sell anything, not a product, not a thought nor an idea. He came to share his love and energy with everyone. During his talk, he forced us to touch each other's face, and hug 7 strangers before going off our evening when his talk finished.

So the big takehome for me was that I really need to re-establish my sense of community and belonging rather than going down a deep rabbit hole and segregating myself from society. There are a lot of people I love who I have pushed away, and I am working on strengthening my energy field to draw them back in, because I need them as much as they need me. I noticed that the first time in a long time, after Zach told me to be more like a flower, live in the moment and roll with the punches so-to-speak, I completely ignored the fact that I was sitting under blue light during the talks, and in the evening while sipping on some wine in the lounge at Shelburne Farms. Of course, I had my blue blockers and skin covered, but normally I would feel stressed and get worked up over this. I decided to be the flower, and just go with it, and to my surprise, I had no issues or concerns. I truly believe that the power of the mind cannot be overlooked when it comes to mitigating blue light and EMF, and while blue blockers and other mitigation strategies should be employed, I believe some individuals can overcome the negative effects with just their own energy alone. Energy balances stress overall, and since energy comes from ANYWHERE in the universe, there are many ways we can harvest it, including ourselves and one another. This was a big lesson for me, and you can tell that Zach mitigates his stress through the immense amount of energy that he holds and spills over for everyone around him.

Kris

### 5G Canary — 2019-06-05T18:08:23+0300

At the end of the day, we are all on the same team. Anyone who cares about their health and is passionate for healing. I think its important to be open, and always show love to others. There's no rivalry.

### kris90 — 2019-06-05T21:24:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vermont-2019.23794/post-272096>

What was also interesting is that not one person had a single bad thing to say about Jack despite his intensity and passion. But even though it's June I still haven't gotten back on my feet from the fallout. There could be a miriad of reasons why, but I was recently thinking about the power of the mind and that balance of control and letting go. Resting in the now and not using it as a mask for actually having given up. I think I've given up recently because my next big step is to get into the sun more. I'm doing everything I can do make that a reality, but I feel down on myself because I don't have the energy for much else in life. But really it's probably all a matter of perspective. > [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=272213)
>
> What to do when friends/family are immersed in nnEMF and incapable of realizing its effect on them.
>
> The people I really relate to anymore are those who live in areas with very little or no cell reception.

I've always been the type of person who has had difficulty achieving balance. When I'm passionate about something, I go "all-in", and that's what I did with my health. I focused on my health for about a decade of my life while everyone who I loved and loved me was basically along for the ride. It was both selfish and selfless of me, because I had to figure out ME first, so I could be the best I could be for THEM.

I've realised I've been healed for close to 3 years now in terms of my health ailments, but I've been living in fear of these ailments returning which I've just come to the conclusion it is not possible. Actually Zach Bush told me it was not possible. So now for the first time ever, I feel like I'm coming up for air and now I'm ready to share my GOOD energy with everyone I love and care about.

So the best advice I can give you both, is worry about yourself FIRST! Being selfish short-term is being selfless long-term. Everyone is on their own journey, and while you may be ahead, there is not much you can do other than focus on YOU and lead by example. Eventually your loved ones will follow, but you must be patient and understand they are on their own path!

I believe you MIGHT need and/or want to go ALL in using the principles Jack teaches here when you are in disease-reversal mode. Honestly, the sooner you can reverse your illness, improve your redox and restore optimal mitochondrial function, the less you have to do to maintain. I've focused MORE than enough time doing so, and it will all depend upon your personal context. I usually assess my clients based on the severity of their illness as well as learning as much as I can about their environment before putting together their optimization plan.

But that being said, once you are optimal, you can learn how to HACK every situation. You learn to let go of fear, and stress or anxiety. You can remain calm in normally difficult situations. You can be enthusiastic even when the weather is shit, or other things aren't going right for you. Once you're there, I believe you really need to establish your sense of community. It's ok if you don't have a lot in common with others. Understand they aren't where you are YET, but they may get there. And if not, help them, not by shoving these principles down their throat, but simply sharing your energy. Don't avoid energy vampires. They need you. They are looking for energy. Share some of your energy to bring them up to a higher energy state so they can use that energy to heal themselves.

I haven't written a book (yet). I have a lot of unpublished content and am working on an e-Course and such. I can keep you posted for sure.

### MKelly — 2019-06-11T17:29:15+0300

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=272213)

>
> The people I really relate to anymore are those who live in areas with very little or no cell reception.

Hi Saichi
Just wanted to check in with you regarding this question. It's tough. Here's how I think about it: with the exception of my children, what other people do is none of my business. Just do what you need to do, get support from the people who are in it with you and don't worry about the others. Lead by example and leave them be. It's tough. I have close friends who are really sick and don't want to hear it. It breaks my heart but it's not up to me. I still treasure my friendship with them. I keep going on my path, and when one day they are open to it, I can plant a seed or share some information, I'll be ready. Just make sure you connect with someone you can talk to about your EMF journey, if you get that need met with someone who's on the same journey it will feel less important to get uninterested people on board. As Kris said in his post, at the beginning we often need a tight focus on our new normal, as it becomes regular normal we can open back up again. It's all good. Stay connected!

My best friend of 30 years in Pittsburgh lost his baby during birth couple years ago. Last year I learned his wife spent a lot of time every day in the playground a block away with their three year old while she was pregnant. When I visited them last I discovered the playground averaged around 12,000 µw/m^2 of RF. I've been trying to convince him to ditch his doctorate program and get back to the land which he's most comfortable with anyways, but he said he's going to wait for a clear signal first. I told him 4G killed his baby, no clearer signal than that. He refuses to talk to me now.

Two of my closer friends in NYC, both Italian, been vegan for a decade and inundated in nnEMF with home and work. They've been hospitalized past couple years and look 10 to 20 years older than me although we're the same age. I believe their consciousness has shrunk to the point of no return.

Most of my Japanese family have already died or are dying from radionuclide EMF. My deadbeat and ignorant brother just became a father, vaccinated my niece and refuses to take her out in the sun which I only know through my mother. I'll probably never meet my niece but perhaps that's best.

I want this dystopia to end. I want a real transformation.

In my Domestic Violence Advocacy Class we are studying Trauma and the Adovocate's Response. Today we discussed positive psychology and well-being. PERMA stands for: Positive Emotions, Engagement, Positive Relationships, Meaning and Accomplishments. All of them count toward well-being. ### Katie Durham — 2019-06-08T18:15:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-lamp-because-scared-of-lightning-and-thunder.23795/post-272282>

### Ed Pomicter — 2019-06-10T01:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-lamp-because-scared-of-lightning-and-thunder.23795/post-272367>

> [Katie Durham said:](/goto/post?id=272282)
>
> You don't want any blue or bright light. That oled light sounds much too bright for nighttime or evening use. How about a very low wattage red LED night light.

The brightness is based upon the "lux" value which is a measurement of illumination with a unit of lumens/sq meter.

The color temperature in degrees Kelvin is a very rough indicator of if the light is more towards the red end of the spectrum (low Kelvin) or towards the blue/white light end of the spectrum (high kelvin), but the spectral distribution is really what shows you the true wavelength outputs of the light source.

The effect on your biological systems is going to be some factor of LUX x TEMP x TIME = EFFECT

So an OLED with a color temp of 1900K "should" be in the reddish range without blue, and if it is a low enough lux, then it would be like a soft candle light.
A Red LED with too much lux could be counter productive depending on the lux X temp x time formula.

And again, it appears that basically any light at night is likely to be problematic.....so perhaps best to work through the fear of thunder and lightening....the thunder won't hurt you and you will never see the lightning bolt that gets you coming!

> I discovered this product also.
> What about the flickering effect of these lamp?
> How do you like the lamps. Satisfied?
>
> I have send the company this email:
>
> "**_Hi, Is there info on the lamps flickering effect? have they been tested for this?"_**
>
> They replied:**_"Thank you for your email. The bulbs use pulse width modulation. The pulse frequency is still well beyond what the eye can detect"_**

PWM is an intrinsic part of the LED and is what is known as flicker. There seems to be an understanding of the upper threshold within the industry but no tests as far as I can find. From a marketing perspective it is surprising that they would not use this as an extra feature of their lights if it was such a benefit they arrived at through planning.

I have another idea for you. A tired paraventricular nucleus makes us prone to be fearful. We share a lot of fears and think them normal, the crime rate, the other side of town is dangerous(said on both sides!) etc . The protocol you have embarked on will raise your fear threshold in time as your PVN and with that your adrenals recover. (adrenal fatigue is not a thing per se though !)

So my suggestion would be this
I am not recommending this as a safe light though, but for nights when your sleep will be interrupted already by the storm that you hear. The remote control at your bedside puts you in control to only switch them on when you cannot stand the storm noises any longer. Keep a cheap non powerful flash light on your bedside table if you want to make doubly sure . Use them with a shade and increase the shading as your journey progresses. Using rechargeable batteries it should be doable. As your journey progresses you will probably need it less and less.
Stick some red foil over these to kill the white light. [https://www.amazon.com/Brilliant-Ev...ess-Control/dp/B01JT65IXE?ref\_=fsclp\_pl\_dp\_15](https://www.amazon.com/Brilliant-Evolution-BRRC134-Wireless-Control/dp/B01JT65IXE?ref_=fsclp_pl_dp_15)

[https://www.amazon.com/Changing-Rul...light+remote&qid=1560343377&s=gateway&sr=8-16](https://www.amazon.com/Changing-RuleaxA-Operated-Function-Wireless/dp/B07SR3SQSK/ref=sr_1_16?keywords=red+led+puck+light+remote&qid=1560343377&s=gateway&sr=8-16)

Don't get tempted to use them every day though.

### Ed Pomicter — 2019-06-12T16:13:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-lamp-because-scared-of-lightning-and-thunder.23795/post-272513>

I have used this tape over things like the display on our stove to block out unwanted wavelengths. I cannot data on find what wavelengths are allowed through, but it is not much since it prevents exposure of photographic materials.

[https://www.amazon.com/3M-Lithographers-Tape-616-Ruby/dp/B0001M6K4M](https://www.amazon.com/3M-Lithographers-Tape-616-Ruby/dp/B0001M6K4M)

### Bob Stirling — 2019-06-12T18:32:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-lamp-because-scared-of-lightning-and-thunder.23795/post-272526>

> Thanks.

Hello Hilde. Yes I like the lamps. They were a replacement for LED so the results were very noticeable. I know flicker is being talked about but have not delved into it. I am using them for morning and late night tasks at high latitudes. The population density is very low so I don't deal with a some of the things that may be experienced in the south.

In regards the brain as a computer, it may find a way to process the flicker more effectively.

### Inger — 2019-06-13T10:16:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-lamp-because-scared-of-lightning-and-thunder.23795/post-272573>

I would watch deeper why you are scared of darkness/lightening and thunder.
To me being in darkness has been very healing. I do use a candle in the winter,months, but I have started to love the darkness now. It forces you to be with you, going deeper inside you, if you let it happen, it is very healing.
When you feel the fear just see if you can let it be, doing nothing, and just open deeper to it. That is how the fear will disappear... at least it did for me

### Hilde — 2019-06-13T10:56:36+0300

# Thread: If you move to the countryside, what profession do you learn before?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-move-to-the-countryside-what-profession-do-you-learn-before.23797/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BrunoB — 2019-06-06T22:47:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-move-to-the-countryside-what-profession-do-you-learn-before.23797/post-272163>

Hello good day. good is that the question. In addition to agriculture,
cattle raising, beekeeping.
I think maybe I could use mechanics for trucks or tractors.
you who think or recommend? thanks

### Bob Stirling — 2019-06-08T06:46:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-move-to-the-countryside-what-profession-do-you-learn-before.23797/post-272262>

Yes mechanics is good, it is always needed. Also operating equipment, loaders, excavators, trucks etc. This would get you outside a lot. I would plan to be self employed at some point. Don't stop at one skill, have 3 or 4. Welding is another one.

### Solidsilverteeth — 2019-06-08T08:55:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-move-to-the-countryside-what-profession-do-you-learn-before.23797/post-272266>

Less than 2% of the worlds population are fishermen or farmers feeding the other 98%

Become a food producer. Either fishing or farming.

Because a small holder, or micro farm or small fishing boat is near the water.

### Seafoodeater — 2020-04-28T19:40:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-move-to-the-countryside-what-profession-do-you-learn-before.23797/post-289114>

Yes learning to provide food for yourself and others will be very valuable skills

Setting nets and shellfish traps, or setting traps on land for small game

Shooting air guns, and then of course foragin wild food will be excellent

Even wild planting guerrilla gardening wild edible plants in unused areas near your home.

Cut and come again healthy nutritious plants that are free and grow wild

# Thread: Hypersensitivity after low carb/Keto due to deuterium?

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MattD1995 — 2019-06-09T20:00:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypersensitivity-after-low-carb-keto-due-to-deuterium.23801/post-272341>

I’ve noticed an interesting phenomena in the low carb/Keto/carnivore communities. Many people who eat these low carb diets often find they have much more aggressive reactions when they attempt to reintroduce high carb foods, alcohol, etc... Clearly, most people in the low carb communities are not getting enough sun, DHA, or CT. Could it be that their mitochondria are actually accumulating deuterium on these low carb/ketogenic diets since they do not have the proper light, water, and magnetism in place?

I’ve noticed that low carb diets seemed to initially make me better but then sicker in the past when I wasn’t performing CT or getting enough sun and DHA. Very curious to hear your thoughts.

# Thread: More on choosing indoor lighting
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-choosing-indoor-lighting.23802/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### metallikat36 — 2019-06-10T03:09:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-choosing-indoor-lighting.23802/post-272372>

FYI, a past thread exists on this too:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...or-you-in-purchasing-artificial-lights.17241/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-goal-for-you-in-purchasing-artificial-lights.17241/)

My impression is that there is no such thing as artificial lighting that is free of problems. I gather some of the considerations are:
\- Flicker?
\- Wavelength distribution exposed to, as a function of the time of day? (i.e. no blue light at night, but okay during day)
\- Ideally no gaps or spikes in the wavelength distribution (i.e. the graph is relatively smooth)
\- toxins like mercury in the bulb?
\- Some say the polarization of the light matters. I have no idea what that means.
\- Should not be dimmable. Not sure why JK mentioned this.

Secondary considerations that don't relate to health are things like:
\- cost of bulb
\- electricity cost
\- heat emitted form bulb
\- color rendering index (CRI)
\- Glare

I use only a lamp on my nightstand with two 40W incandescent bulbs in them. I know incandescents provide wavelenghts that are close to fire, which has little blue light, and no gaps or spikes in the spectrum. Those are the good things.

Do incandescents flicker? I have read contradictory info on this?

Check out this 2011 article in Popular Mechanics. They tested the wavelengths distribution of a variety of incandescents, CFLs, and LEDs:
[https://www.popularmechanics.com/te...ent-vs-led-ultimate-light-bulb-test/?slide=18](https://www.popularmechanics.com/technology/gadgets/reviews/g164/incandescent-vs-compact-fluorescent-vs-led-ultimate-light-bulb-test/?slide=18)
Incandescents seem to provide the best CRI as well as wavelengths.

Apparently, a candle is still has slightly less blue light than incandescents:
[https://www.sunlightinside.com/light-and-health/light-for-sleep/](https://www.sunlightinside.com/light-and-health/light-for-sleep/)

Here are three products that may be of interest I have run into:

**1\. Sunlight Inside:**
[https://www.sunlightinside.com/light-and-health/comparing-philips-hue-natural-light/](https://www.sunlightinside.com/light-and-health/comparing-philips-hue-natural-light/)
This appears to be a new company. They don't even appear to sell replacement bulbs yet. It looks like it's LED lighting that automatically changes in 24 hour cycles to mimic the phases of the sun during the day, and fire at night. It is expensive, but the wavelength distributions seems good. Not sure about other issues like flicker though:
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**2\. Bedtime Bulb:**
[https://www.amazon.com/Bedtime-Low-Blue-Light-Healthy-Sleep/dp/B07H49N46N](https://www.amazon.com/Bedtime-Low-Blue-Light-Healthy-Sleep/dp/B07H49N46N)
This claims to eliminate flicker. Not sure how, or if it is true at all. Wavelengths look good though:

It's only 350 lumens though, so multiple bulbs may be needed to achieve one's goals.

**3\. Night Switch Bulb**
[https://www.amazon.com/Enriched-Depleted-Nighttime-Optimized-Circadian/dp/B077S1W8XG/](https://www.amazon.com/Enriched-Depleted-Nighttime-Optimized-Circadian/dp/B077S1W8XG/)
This has one mode for day:

And one mode for night:

So what do you guys think? We have incandescents, Sunlight Inside, Bright Bulb, and Nite Switch Bulb. These options all seem to pass the wavelength test. Surprisingly incandescents seem to have the most blue of these four options. But what about other issues like flicker, toxins, and polarization? I really don't know what's best. In the other thread Jack suggested a 12 volt DC reflector lamp. I am clueless about those.

### metallikat36 — 2019-06-12T10:38:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-choosing-indoor-lighting.23802/post-272492>

Other possible contenders:

GoodDay and GoodNight bulbs. Sold separately. No obvious info on wavelengths. Some reviews complained of flicker:
[https://www.amazon.com/Lighting-Science-FG-02609-GoodDay-HealthE/dp/B06XBZLGDZ](https://www.amazon.com/Lighting-Science-FG-02609-GoodDay-HealthE/dp/B06XBZLGDZ)
[https://www.amazon.com/product-revi...yStar=five\_star&showViewpoints=0&pageNumber=1](https://www.amazon.com/product-reviews/B018YIK1X0/ref=cm_cr_arp_d_hist_5?ie=UTF8&filterByStar=five_star&showViewpoints=0&pageNumber=1)
I think the same company might sell a "Genesis Lamp" that is like Sunlight Inside in that it changes on its own between day and night. Qualitatively, the Sunlight Inside light looks/feels better to me, at least in the photos I found.

Lohas bulb:
[https://www.amazon.com/LOHAS-LED-Goodnight-Replacement-Non-Dimmable/dp/B07FR33F6V/ref=sr\_1\_1\_sspa?crid=3K7YKN4GYYER5&keywords=goodnight+bulb&qid=1560324253&s=gateway&sprefix=goodnight+bul,aps,187&sr=8-1-spons&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/LOHAS-LED-Goodnight-Replacement-Non-Dimmable/dp/B07FR33F6V/ref=sr_1_1_sspa?crid=3K7YKN4GYYER5&keywords=goodnight+bulb&qid=1560324253&s=gateway&sprefix=goodnight+bul%2Caps%2C187&sr=8-1-spons&psc=1)
Cheap and comes in a 2-pack. The blue free wavelengths still contain a violet spike, which is unnatural for after dark, but I don't know if that is of consequence. Also still contains some marginal green wavelengths.

Sleep-shift light:
[https://www.amazon.com/Sleep-Shift-...nin-Production/dp/B018S18860?tag=biohkrlab-20](https://www.amazon.com/Sleep-Shift-Naturally-Supports-Melatonin-Production/dp/B018S18860?tag=biohkrlab-20)

One suspicious thing is that some products contain 1 or 2 exact duplicate product images. Might be stock images. Or might be same product branded differently. Just something to keep an eye open for.

At the moment, I am inclined to try the "Night Switch bulb". Getting some blue during the day might be good, and the nighttime wavelengths clearly have no blue at all, assuming their graph is truthful. One reviewer complained that it forgets it's last setting after a while, so that you might sometimes turn it on at night, but get they daytime wavelenghts, and have to toggle to the next setting to get to the nighttime setting. I can imaging getting up to pee at night and this happening. Not sure if a 1 second blast of blue is enough to upset things though.

### metallikat36 — 2019-06-12T10:55:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-choosing-indoor-lighting.23802/post-272493>

And there are apps to test flicker:
[https://www.soraa.com/learn/science/how-test-led-flicker-home.php](https://www.soraa.com/learn/science/how-test-led-flicker-home.php)
I will report back on any new bulb I get, as well as my current incandescents.

### Lahelada — 2019-06-12T17:41:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-choosing-indoor-lighting.23802/post-272519>

[https://www.derlichtpeter.de/en/](https://www.derlichtpeter.de/en/)
Lichtpeter has already done some heavy lifting for you in terms of flicker only not blue light !

> [metallikat36 said:](/goto/post?id=272372)
>
> I use only a lamp on my nightstand with two 40W incandescent bulbs in them

Way too much at night. One red 15W incandescent would be borderline in this setting.

> [metallikat36 said:](/goto/post?id=272372)
>
> It's only **350 lumens** though, so multiple bulbs may be needed to achieve one's goals.

The oal is to use no light,the next best thing VERY little light,another next best thing does not exist. We hit the bad for you range...

> [metallikat36 said:](/goto/post?id=272492)
>
> The blue free wavelengths still contain a violet spike, which is unnatural for after dark, but I don't know if that is of consequence. Also still contains some marginal green wavelengths.

It is of consequence. Each colour has a tolerance threshold expressed in studies in lux. Lumen and Kelvin are misleading for our purposes. Your 350 lumen output would mean that you can put one of those lamps in a 300 sq ft room and live in the far corner from the lamp in the other corner
only. The effect on melatonin release is present in illumination over 10 lux ( lux is defined as light that hits you)

> [metallikat36 said:](/goto/post?id=272492)
>
> Sleep-shift light:

630 lumen ...

### Lahelada — 2019-06-12T17:41:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-choosing-indoor-lighting.23802/post-272520>

[https://www.rapidtables.com/calc/light/lumen-to-lux-calculator.html](https://www.rapidtables.com/calc/light/lumen-to-lux-calculator.html)

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-06-12T18:29:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-choosing-indoor-lighting.23802/post-272525>

I agree that artificial lighting on the market, at least for now, **sucks in one way or another.**

Our ancestral lighting was sun and fire. It behooves us to stay as close to our evolutionary path as possible.

Not all of us can, but if you install a fireplace or wood burning stove (where fire is visible) you are ahead of the game, at least when such heating is needed (not so much in warm summers).

If you can afford it (especially if you are handy with tools to DIY), light tubes installed in walls or roof are next best thing to living in sun. Once purchase/installation costs are paid back (through lower electricity costs), you have essentially free lighting forever.

After dark you will need of course supplementary lighting. But our ancestors tended to wind down their activities and go to bed soon after dark anyway.

Another factor to consider in the lighting equation is color/shade of walls and ceilings. That will influence overall color ambience (I have seen a few horrid color schemes that are solid strong blue or some other peculiar one-off color-- not good in my opinion).

I think we would likely do best color-wise with a neutral, very pastel off-white color of some hue.

### Lahelada — 2019-06-12T18:44:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-choosing-indoor-lighting.23802/post-272529>

>
> I think we would likely do best color-wise with a neutral, very pastel off-white color of some hue.

>
> Sleep-shift light:
> [https://www.amazon.com/Sleep-Shift-...nin-Production/dp/B018S18860?tag=biohkrlab-20](https://www.amazon.com/Sleep-Shift-Naturally-Supports-Melatonin-Production/dp/B018S18860?tag=biohkrlab-20)
>
>
> . I can imaging getting up to pee at night and this happening. Not sure if a 1 second blast of blue is enough to upset things though.

I believe 1 second is enough to eff up rhythm and affect sleep/hormones and general wellness. If there is even a small chance that it will blast me with bright junk light or flicker, i wont even plug it in. At night, when we have been in the dark for a while, our light receptors absorb light like a sponge. An amount of light that is imperceptible during daylight is blinding and can trigger big changes at night. I havent stubbed my toes since ive given up light, actually can see/sense more overall.

### metallikat36 — 2019-06-13T02:11:03+0300

### metallikat36 — 2019-06-15T23:14:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-choosing-indoor-lighting.23802/post-272709>

Update:
\- The Bedtime Bulb guy said in an interview his bulb tested the lowest flicker of any bulb he's ever tested. He say it's spec'd at "less than 1%", but that every bulb so far measured has come up less than 0.1%. What measure of flicker he is using I do not know, but that sounds promising.
\- He's also posted a list of compatible dimmers. So if 350 lumens is too much for folk, it can likely be dimmed without issue.
\- In any case, he says he's calculated the melanopic lumens of the bulb to be less than any on the market.
\- I've ordered one bulb. Will update.

### metallikat36 — 2019-06-17T02:06:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-choosing-indoor-lighting.23802/post-272751>

The bulb is nice. I like the color very much. Holding my smart phone camera up to the bulb does show some lines moving across the screen, presumably indicating some flickering. But it looked a lot less "violent", for lack of a better word than the previous incandescent I had. I tried the bulb in different sockets in the house and the horizontal lines looked different depending on the socket. I wonder if his claims to be virtually flicker-free might be true, and that maybe it is just my homes electrical that is causing it. In any case, the bulb "feels" great to me. I'm gonna get more.

### Der Lichtpeter — 2019-06-28T17:05:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-choosing-indoor-lighting.23802/post-273415>

> [metallikat36 said:](/goto/post?id=272560)
>
> Wow, thanks. This is a good resource:
> [https://www.derlichtpeter.de/en/light-flicker/market-tests/](https://www.derlichtpeter.de/en/light-flicker/market-tests/)
> I am surprised that many LEDs have <1% CFD, but no incandescents do. I'm going to email him. It seems he would be willing to test for flicker a handful of LED bulbs that don't have blue light, if we pay for them. I'd be willing to chip in $25 for a bulb.

Incandescents running on European mains power supply do flicker with a CFD of about 10%. 10% is an amount which never has caused any troubles in the past. There are a few listed in the market-tests, please search for 'IncStd' or 'IncHalo' and you will find them. Running on 12V AC the CFD is of about 2%, because the tungsten filament is a lot thicker and thus much higher is the thermal inertia.

### metallikat36 — 2019-06-28T23:48:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-choosing-indoor-lighting.23802/post-273440>

There are a few listed in the market-tests, please search for 'IncStd' or 'IncHalo' and you will find them. Running on 12V AC the CFD is of about 2%, because the tungsten filament is a lot thicker and thus much higher is the thermal inertia.

Thank you for posting. If you ever become interested in testing flicker for low-blue-light products, there are definitely a handful of contenders in the US. The tentative best combo of low melanopic lumens and hopefully low flicker that isn't super expensive seems to be Bedtime Bulb.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-07-30T22:21:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-choosing-indoor-lighting.23802/post-275204>

Ok, everyone, I'm serious. We need to talk...........about the light you allow around you as you live.

It's time for you to see the light.

Actually, its time for you to not see the light at night.

In this study of elderly adults, artificial light at night of just over 5 LUX, increased their risk of [#diabetes](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/diabetes?source=feed_text&epa=HASHTAG&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARBXwqAJNeL3AKv0P83UCTO8XIZ3QctDs00JcgfFg87JMz0ZnEh-KFho5IkP6TM3kCGorfITUb-UH6qsqrfo3O4HS0qZrJolte_Ua9jQWvwZrYThZiyws6kcRRPIUu66f4OVD2CGM5rF7F0RluEmtEFrLGNFjQtNWxDrafHaPPQWPcJ4eUayhpZBePchq_6kz1xRmMRvCEkqVgHvu_ss9HgXGRz7LbU28ASOZYHV1NY_VNK03NsGuhX6mubGsoGOttGlhxbMvf7-kmb0EPnPrCWkDoH5Bn6F7DBVc8Or4A8owuklk7TRxhgk97f293gyB60hEHwGNuHCywvvENUrK6f_j1QpXT6UovbAvLi5bbiT8pifJS6ge-Hrugc&__tn__=%2ANK-R) by three times.

Even at 3 lux, the results were significant. An on/off blinker light on one of your electronic devices can generate three lux of light.

Continually exposing yourself to light at night is kind of like eating cupcakes 24/7.

Put the light cupcake down.

[#MakeLUXLowAgain](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/makeluxlowagain?source=feed_text&epa=HASHTAG&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARBXwqAJNeL3AKv0P83UCTO8XIZ3QctDs00JcgfFg87JMz0ZnEh-KFho5IkP6TM3kCGorfITUb-UH6qsqrfo3O4HS0qZrJolte_Ua9jQWvwZrYThZiyws6kcRRPIUu66f4OVD2CGM5rF7F0RluEmtEFrLGNFjQtNWxDrafHaPPQWPcJ4eUayhpZBePchq_6kz1xRmMRvCEkqVgHvu_ss9HgXGRz7LbU28ASOZYHV1NY_VNK03NsGuhX6mubGsoGOttGlhxbMvf7-kmb0EPnPrCWkDoH5Bn6F7DBVc8Or4A8owuklk7TRxhgk97f293gyB60hEHwGNuHCywvvENUrK6f_j1QpXT6UovbAvLi5bbiT8pifJS6ge-Hrugc&__tn__=%2ANK-R) (at night)

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1389945719302436](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1389945719302436)

# Thread: Dry and chapped lower lib

### Antonis — 2019-06-10T06:59:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-and-chapped-lower-lib.23803/post-272378>

Hello guys, hope you all doing well with your lifes. I got an issue with my libs the last week.
Since I started my lifeguard job at a beach area where is windy, my lower lib got dry and chapped. Its a bit painful too. Have you ever experienced such a thing ? If so, what can I do for that to heal? Am in the sun everyday the whole day. I might burn my libs that's why I have this issue. But anyways, I would really appreciate your help, thanks.

### Katie Durham — 2019-06-11T22:18:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-and-chapped-lower-lib.23803/post-272472>

Antonis, is your lip burned or just dry and chapped? A natural oil like coconut or olive oil should be soothing and healing. If you need protection from all day sun, then your healthiest option might be something like zinc oxide. I don't know if our lips are supposed to develop a sun callus like the rest of our body from chronic exposure to early morning sun. Perhaps.

### Antonis — 2019-06-12T10:36:40+0300

I don't know if our lips are supposed to develop a sun callus like the rest of our body from chronic exposure to early morning sun. Perhaps.

It started healing now. I think it was from the advanced sun exposure. I was just worried but I like it. I might burn my lib for the first time but the benefits are amazing. I feel superhero since am spending the whole day in the sun being productive too. I got a subtropical tan aswel lol. Thanks for your advice. I was actually using the olive oil to smooth it. Thanks!!

### drezy — 2019-06-13T02:15:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-and-chapped-lower-lib.23803/post-272561>

[@Antonis](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20215/)

Just for your reference.

Last year I spent almost all summer outside UVindex 12-13.

I that found this year that when the solar power consistently crossed the UVi 9 mark or so my nose skin got flaky for about a week and then settled into a nice tan. It sounds similar to your experience, though you are likely taking some more wind than I am too.

### Antonis — 2019-06-13T07:12:31+0300

I guess so. You were probably spending the whole day too. This year am spending the most in the sun with no break. There is a little wound on my lib that is trying to heal. Let's see. I applied avocado oil. Thanks man!!

### drezy — 2019-06-13T09:13:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-and-chapped-lower-lib.23803/post-272569>

> [Antonis said:](/goto/post?id=272568)
>
> I guess so. You were probably spending the whole day too. This year am spending the most in the sun with no break. There is a little wound on my lib that is trying to heal. Let's see. I applied avocado oil. Thanks man!!

Good luck.

That's what confused me a little earlier this year. Since I spent 3-4 days a week outside between 7AM to 8PM (barring some food shopping and cooking) I was surprised that my nose flaked up this year. It seemed to sort itself out pretty quickly.

Hopefully you'll heal right back up.

### Antonis — 2019-06-13T11:10:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-and-chapped-lower-lib.23803/post-272575>

Thanks bro!

### Sean Waters — 2019-06-16T17:12:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-and-chapped-lower-lib.23803/post-272731>

FWIW I found that my nose and forehead were the only areas of my body to get burnt and real sore my last few trips to Equator... India, Sri Lanka, Mexico... everytime, it is my nose and forehead.

I was also using a smartphone on all of these holidays.

Getting rid of my smartphone recently cut all the blue light from the equation.

My nose and forehead have stopped being so badly burnt now... still not perfect.

I think 10 years of staring at a screen has probably destroyed the Redox in the skin in those areas. Effectively, rendering those Mitochondria "senescent". The Skin is meant to regen every 48-72 hours. So, it would explain why my whole body goes beautifully brown and never burns, but the area with the most BL Exposure in my whole life = Red, flaky, chapped and sore.

I'd also be aware of using PC/ Laptop or if you also don't use a smartphone. Iris and F.lux are good, but it is **still artificial light.** The more I'm learning, the less I think the "light issue" is purely a case of cutting the Blue. I think, anything that is **non-native, no matter how "safe", if it is electric and I think it will have _a damaging effect on Mitochondria.
_**

# Thread: Feeling better
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-better.23808/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thora Pomicter — 2019-06-11T15:11:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-better.23808/post-272444>

I don't know if it is because I have relaxed more about my sleep or if it is all of the hacks I have been doing but last night, I did wake 3 times but I was able to go right back to sleep. I woke up feeling rested this morning. I also have more energy during the days lately, although I still get tired after being in my crappy work environment. I just make sure that when I get home, I spend lots of time outside with my bare feet on the ground and most of my clothes off.

We are still shooting for the end of summer to get my factory moved back home where I will be out of that toxic environment. I am also very proud of myself for consistently easing into the cold thermogenesis. I am hoping that by the winter, I will have adapted enough to keep it up and maybe I won't be so uncomfortable in the winter. Cold is not my favorite thing by any stretch. My husband put a galvanized steel tub that fits the two of us right next to our pool, so we can go from the ice tub to the pool. Taking ice baths outside is much nicer than being inside in the bathtub.

Happy to be making progress. I have a very strong motivation to make myself as well as I can be, as I watch my mother decline more and more. She has dementia that is getting worse, macular degeneration, hearing loss, general weakness...I just look ar her as a mitochondrial train wreck and I am determined not to end up that way. It is sad to see her decline and the worst part is that she has no motivation to do anything to make herself better. My husband and I have tried over the years, but unless you are driven to help yourself, there is nothing that anyone else can do to make a difference.

### caroline — 2019-06-12T06:02:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-better.23808/post-272489>

C/T is soooooo much better outside! My C/T tank is the pacific ocean!

### Guilherme — 2019-06-11T22:44:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-days-dry-fasting.23811/post-272475>

I will begin my vacation soon and was thinking in doing a long dry fast(no water) given the supposedly benefits to health.

I already have been doing Intermediate fasting for 5 months, with the last meal at noon.

I plan to do this passing most of the say at the beach. I would like to know if there is actually a science based objection to a dry fast and if should just stick to 7 days normal fast?

### Katie Durham — 2019-06-12T23:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-days-dry-fasting.23811/post-272550>

### Guilherme — 2019-06-13T00:27:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-days-dry-fasting.23811/post-272556>

Only by feeling, if I feel I'm not well I will break the fast.

Have you considered instead to do a urine fast? Far safer.

Have a read of these books.

ORIN THERAPY (urine therapy) click on these to download the entire books for free. (I recomend your own perfect medicine to start, then the water of life book)

[Your-Own-Perfect-Medicine-](http://aquariusthewaterbearer.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/10/Your-Own-Perfect-Medicine-by-Martha-Christy-with-Testimonials.pdf) by Martha Christy with Testimonials

[Urine Therapy](http://aquariusthewaterbearer.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/10/Urine-Therapy-by-Flora-Peschek-B%C3%B6hmer-1.pdf) by Flora Peschek-Böhmer

[MORARJI DESAI ON CBS EVENING NEWS WITH DAN RATHER](http://aquariusthewaterbearer.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/10/MORARJI-DESAI-ON-CBS-EVENING-NEWS-WITH-DAN-RATHER.rtf) – Former Prime Miinister of India discusses Urine Therapy

### Sue-UK — 2019-06-13T12:40:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-days-dry-fasting.23811/post-272576>

> [Guilherme said:](/goto/post?id=272475)
>
> I will begin my vacation soon and was thinking in doing a long dry fast(no water) given the supposedly benefits to health.
>
> I already have been doing Intermediate fasting for 5 months, with the last meal at noon.
>
> I plan to do this passing most of the say at the beach. I would like to know if there is actually a science based objection to a dry fast and if should just stick to 7 days normal fast?

Hi Guilherme

If you are here for Jack's teachings, and by posting the thread in the beginners forum, I assume you still consider yourself a beginner in terms of his teachings, dry fasting isn't one of 6 healers [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/) Nor is under drinking which has been talked about a lot recently, and (re SST's post on urine) we haven't been taught that drinking our own urine counts as unfluoridated water under healer number 2.

I might naturally dry fast overnight for 16 hours at winter solstice, and 8 hours by summer solstice. A hibernating bear might go 5 months dry fasting. For me, each natural scenario says cold (or at least cooler), dark and boring. i.e. there are daily and seasonal rhythms to dry fasting, involving _absence_ of sunlight. I'd need to carefully think about that before doing _any_ sort of hack involving dry fasting, but particularly one that involved an extended dry fast while on the beach in _midsummer._ In terms of risk/benefit ratio, that's a different ballgame.

I have never heard anybody say saliva is a waste product. Unless you spit a lot, then about 100% of saliva remains in the body, how could this be a channel to get bad things out? That doesn't make any sense to me.

Out breath makes total sense that the body uses this Chanel to get bad things out. 24/7 you are breathing out, the lungs are actually the primary toxic eliminator.

Your poop and skin and mucus membrains are also ways the body gets bad things out.

But urine is like saliva where about 92-99.5% goes back into the body. This can't be a way to get bad things out of the body, that just doesn't make any sense.

This amazing substance that has been regulated and made perfect by your kidneys goes back into the bloodstream and only a fraction just a few percent of the excess goes to your bladder. ### Solidsilverteeth — 2019-06-14T18:48:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-days-dry-fasting.23811/post-272641>

The function of the kidneys is to keep the various elements in your blood balanced. The kidneys do not filter out important elements in the blood because those elements in themselves are toxic or poisonous or bad for the body, but simply because the body did not need that particular concentration of that element at the time it was excreted.

And medical researchers have discovered that many of the elements of the blood that are found in urine have enormous medicinal value, and when they are reintroduced into the body, they boost the body's immune defenses and stimulate healing in a way that nothing else does.

As medical research has revealed:

"One of the most important functions of the kidney is to excrete material and substances for which the body has no immediate need..."

\- Urinalysis in Clinical and Laboratory Practice

For instance, the kidneys filter out water and sodium from the blood into the urine. Are water and sodium toxic? Of course not, they're both vital life-sustaining elements without which your body cannot function. But both elements could be lethal if there were too much water or sodium in your blood.

Now what about potassium, calcium, and magnesium - these are familiar nutrients that we ingest in our food and vitamin pills everyday but they're also in your urine. These nutritional elements are extremely valuable substances to the body, certainly not toxic, and yet the kidney excretes these elements into the urine - why? Because it's taking out the excess amount of the potassium, calcium, etc. that is not needed at thatMtime that they are filtered out. Actually, it is this regulating process of the kidneys and the excretion of urine that allows us to eat and drink more than our bodies need at any one time.

"The principal function of the kidney is not excretion, but regulation The kidney obviously conserves what we need, but even snore, permits us the freedom of excess. That is, it allows us to take in more than ... we need of many necessities – water and salt for example – and excretes exactly what is not required."

— Dr. S. Cameron Prof. of Renal Medicine Guy's Hospital, London

But this isn't the end of the story. Scientists have discovered that urine, because it is actually extracted from our blood, contains small amounts of almost all of the life-sustaining nutrients, proteins, hormones, antibodies and immunizing agents that our blood contains:

"Urine can be regarded as one of the most complex of all body fluids. It contains practically all of the constituents found in the blood."

— Urinalysis in Clinical and Laboratory Practice

Taken from page 20 of this book

[http://aquariusthewaterbearer.com/w...icine-by-Martha-Christy-with-Testimonials.pdf](http://aquariusthewaterbearer.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/10/Your-Own-Perfect-Medicine-by-Martha-Christy-with-Testimonials.pdf)

Yes this is what I found works best for me, 5pm until 6pm one meal, on one plate in one hour, with one caloric beverage. Then I dry fast for 12 hrs until 6am. At 6am I drink pure distilled structurised water while grounded and watching the sunrise.

I was the same as you JanSz to start with, i said I could never have the will power to do OMAD. First I went TMAD, which is 2 meals a day, 18:6 fasting, then I slowly reduced to a 4 hour eating window, then 2 hour now I'm at 1 hour eating window and it's really not hard. Just keep busy.

The other secret to success is orin looping. This really makes it easier, I know it's hard to believe until you experience it yourself, but your own ultra filtered blood plasma water really does sustain you without taking you out of a fasted state.

It's the only drink you can have that does not break your fast and you feel full and sustained. It's full of nutrition and good things but is not needed to be digested because it's already exactly the same as what is in your blood.

It's fully structured water. More structured than any other water on planet earth.

And with morning and evening sun you can improve the quality of your own metabolic water even more.

This video is excellent, he sums up what we in the OMAD orin looping community have been doing for years, and it really fits in with Dr Cruse teachings

If someone is not in good health because they haven't eaten enough,or the body _perceives_ they haven't eaten enough (such as leptin resistance), the stressor is _not_ putting food in. In sleep science it is also known that not eating enough can have a negative impact on sleep.

> [Solidsilverteeth said:](/goto/post?id=272869)
>
> Yes this is what I found works best for me, 5pm until 6pm one meal, on one plate in one hour, with one caloric beverage. Then I dry fast for 12 hrs until 6am. At 6am I drink pure distilled structurised water while grounded and watching the sunrise.
>
> I was the same as you JanSz to start with, i said I could never have the will power to do OMAD. First I went TMAD, which is 2 meals a day, 18:6 fasting, then I slowly reduced to a 4 hour eating window, then 2 hour now I'm at 1 hour eating window and it's really not hard. Just keep busy.
>
> The other secret to success is orin looping. This really makes it easier, I know it's hard to believe until you experience it yourself, but your own ultra filtered blood plasma water really does sustain you without taking you out of a fasted state.
>
> It's the only drink you can have that does not break your fast and you feel full and sustained. It's full of nutrition and good things but is not needed to be digested because it's already exactly the same as what is in your blood.
>
> It's fully structured water. More structured than any other water on planet earth.
>
> And with morning and evening sun you can improve the quality of your own metabolic water even more.
>
> This video is excellent, he sums up what we in the OMAD orin looping community have been doing for years, and it really fits in with Dr Cruse teachings
>
>

According to Light in Shaping Life (one of the suggested books here), how the meal that first breaks a fast is partitioned in the body is different to subsequent meals in a day. It is partitioned to support further activity - an important timing consideration to someone with say chronic fatigue, or suffering from brain fog. Drinking urine to avoid breaking a fast, or using willpower or trying to keep busy to avoid eating when hungry could lead to a worsening of health for someone, or become someone's else's eating disorder. That's apart from the papers you've been pointed to about urine not being sterile, contaminants such as fluoride, aluminium etc etc.

Jack has not taught that urine is more structured than any other water on planet earth, or advocated drinking it, and whether even OMAD is a good idea depends on n=1 context. Your own one meal between 5 and 6 pm goes against the teaching for several months of the year in the UK - it is after sunset.

### Lahelada — 2019-06-19T13:33:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-days-dry-fasting.23811/post-272922>

"When fat is broken down during sleep in your body, it turns into water and carbon dioxide. The water is in the form of sweat and urine. The sweat cools you down to increase your mitochondria’s magnetic sense. **Urine is old recycled water that has poor electron carrying capacity**. It is less dense than your blood plasma and your CSF. Less dense water is expelled electrically and magnetically by your kidneys. The more dense water is the more electrons it can carry. CSF is more dense by design. This happens by the action of your choroid plexus in your brain. This is why in cold temperatures make you urinate quickly. You are ridding your body of less dense water and structuring more dense water in your CSF naturally. This cycle occurs via water carried in your blood plasma. This is how you structure your own water naturally."
Blog:Tensegrity 5

### Marko Pollo — 2019-06-19T18:01:03+0300

From " Nourishment, what aminals can teach us about nutritional wisdom" by fred provenza, a wildlife behavior biologist with a lifetime of field work.

"In over 40 years of working with sheep. I have never seen one eat soil or feces or lick up urine patches."

He goes on to explain that only nutrient deficit animals will ever lick a urine patch, and it is a learned behavior, not instinctual. Perhaps as he suggests, one who drinks urine is unknowingly trying to fill a nutritional void like a phosphorus deficiency. Not a fan of supplements, but i think id choose a multivitamin with a glass of water.

I cant speak for all animals, only the ones ive observed. My dogs, who are the least squeamish, will eat chicken, horse, sheep poop, phlegm, rotton food but never urine. I pee arround them all the time. None have any interest. They all avoid grass with pee.

This is not an attack on the pee drinkers, but an opposing view for those who are being encouraged to drink pee. I watched a bit of a video you posted and the doctor stated a doctor in the us will lose their license for even discussing urine therapy. Perhaps this site isnt the best forum to promote pee drinking.

Cheers

# Thread: Pregnant Mitochondriac!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnant-mitochondriac.23812/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sunny20152019 — 2019-06-12T01:09:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnant-mitochondriac.23812/post-272477>

Hi! I've been researching and following Dr. Kruse for the last few months! I'm 25 weeks pregnant and have been either working out outside during the sunrise or watching it (belly exposed) with my 4 year old just about every morning! We are also in the morning sun as much as we can and in small bursts throughout the day (belly exposed). We all have our blue blocking glasses on at night, only 30 minutes of tv per day, wifi only turned on when we need it, limited cell phone use (absolutely no cell phone use for my 4 year old), use the Iris app on our computer (which we rarely use), changed out all the light bulbs to amber Edison bulbs, biohacked our bathroom lights, and mainly rely on salt lamps for our nighttime light.
I am the general manager of a sushi restaurant, so I eat a good amount of seafood (best quality) for DHA, eat a variety of seaweeds, eat a bunch of avocados, cook with coconut oil, we have chickens so we have eggs as much as possible, we only drink spring water and eat mostly fruits and vegetables that are in season at our latitude and local organic, grass fed meats.
Does anyone have any suggestions on other things to do during and after pregnancy to ensure my unborn daughter has the best redox potential she can?
Thanks!!!

### LieselK — 2019-07-24T22:32:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnant-mitochondriac.23812/post-274952>

> [Sunny20152019 said:](/goto/post?id=272477)
>
> Hi! I've been researching and following Dr. Kruse for the last few months! I'm 25 weeks pregnant and have been either working out outside during the sunrise or watching it (belly exposed) with my 4 year old just about every morning! We are also in the morning sun as much as we can and in small bursts throughout the day (belly exposed). We all have our blue blocking glasses on at night, only 30 minutes of tv per day, wifi only turned on when we need it, limited cell phone use (absolutely no cell phone use for my 4 year old), use the Iris app on our computer (which we rarely use), changed out all the light bulbs to amber Edison bulbs, biohacked our bathroom lights, and mainly rely on salt lamps for our nighttime light.
> I am the general manager of a sushi restaurant, so I eat a good amount of seafood (best quality) for DHA, eat a variety of seaweeds, eat a bunch of avocados, cook with coconut oil, we have chickens so we have eggs as much as possible, we only drink spring water and eat mostly fruits and vegetables that are in season at our latitude and local organic, grass fed meats.
> Does anyone have any suggestions on other things to do during and after pregnancy to ensure my unborn daughter has the best redox potential she can?
> Thanks!!!

Sounds like you're doing great! How do you feel? Did you start a journal on the forum? You're getting so close now! What are your plans for birth?

### Sunny20152019 — 2019-07-25T02:17:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnant-mitochondriac.23812/post-274969>

Thank you! I feel great mostly! My hips get a little sore after doing my bootcamp class or working a double on the concrete floors at my restaurant... but that happened with my first as well. We are still in the sun as much as possible and watching the sunrise! I feel less tired throughout the day and after I go outside for twenty minutes and soak in the rays. With the higher fat, higher protein diet, I'm not getting cravings for anything really. Sleep is great! I don't wake up at all unless I have to use the restroom.. which is all the time during the day now...
I gave birth naturally in a hospital with my first and didn't enjoy the experience. (They gave me plitocin in my IV without informing me after giving birth.) We have a new. licensed birthing center where I live that opened a few years ago that we are attending. We absolutely love it there and I plan to have a water birth if everything goes accordingly! Thanks for asking!
I'm not online too often, usually only to check emails or listen to podcasts occasionally, so I don't think I would be good at keeping up with an online journal.

### LieselK — 2019-09-13T02:48:31+0300

### Sunny20152019 — 2019-09-13T03:51:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnant-mitochondriac.23812/post-277763>

Feeling great and comfortable at almost 39 weeks! I’m pretty sure I owe it to the sun! My son and I watch the sunrise every day before he goes to school, I hang out in my garden and walk my trail (belly exposed) during the day, then my husband, son and I hang out outside and play in the sprinkler most nights! Just waiting for baby #2 to make her grand entrance! Thanks for asking!

### Sunny20152019 — 2019-09-30T04:49:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnant-mitochondriac.23812/post-279225>

I delivered her via water birth on Monday, September 23rd (her due date).. my water had broken at 4:30pm with no contractions and it took a few hours to get them going. So of course we walked outside for a couple miles during the sunset! At 8:30pm, I went into active labor and had her an hour later at 9:25pm! It was an amazing experience! Waaayyy less traumatic than in the hospital! And she’s been sleeping 4 hour stretches at night and LOVES/prefers to be outside. If she’s inside too long, she gets fussy ☺️
We started watching the sunrise on day 2 of life! Day one it was storming outside ‍♀️

I loved learning what Dr. K teaches and it's been incredibly helpful. Lately
I've also been returning to the psychological aspects of health and somehow the understanding of
light manifesting as matter has made me understand more about how our WORDS also manifest
our reality. I was into this 20 years ago but then let it fade from my focus ... now I think it's an
absolutely vital part of our well-being (and I'm not referring to religion, so don't let that part turn
you off) ... this is not about religion but about the physics of light and sound and words - how they
literally create what we experience. And yes, I call that God. Here is a very old but excellent audio recording
"Your Word is Your Wand"

# Thread: high desert (or maybe low)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-desert-or-maybe-low.23816/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### StylesGrant — 2019-06-12T22:29:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-desert-or-maybe-low.23816/post-272542>

One of the themes in a lot of environmental illness groups is a 'location effect' which kruse has his own idea of as well. I havent been to yucatan or the equator unfortunately. Although I do seem to become drastically drastically better in the high desert or the southwest. I would imagine the baja coast would really have a good impact, probably the best of both worlds. I really only feel well directly on the coast/beach or the desert. I noticed I slept near a meteor impact preservation historic site deal in arizona and that really seemed pronounced. I slept like 3 hours and felt like I had days of sleep. I thought that was weird.

In any case, in raw blunt terms the sheer brightness of barstow and the majave desert is the best natural anti depressant I've encountered.

### JanSz — 2019-06-13T18:15:51+0300

1\. it is located in tropics
2\. it have higher magnetic fields
...
Possibly you are affected by magnetic fields.

# Thread: The Girl Who Lived
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Vervaina — 2019-06-13T21:22:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-272597>

(this title is for my fellow Potterheads…)

Hello to anyone reading this!!!

I have been out of the loop since last fall.

I was upgraded to stage 4 cancer (mets to the bones) and so I went offline (except to pay bills and necessary emails).

My online time is still very limited.

update - Dr Gordon (amazing surgeon) performed my mastectomy in July and it was later found (in November) that I had gotten a bad CT scan before the surgery I was already stage 4 before I went under the knife.

I have been all over the country (and now even to Europe) for latest treatment and info. My journey has even lead me to get treatment at the famed Hallwang Klinic in Germany.

Cancer research and info seems to change on a daily basis right now and much of the info seems contradictory.​

I would like to post some of what the top cancer docs have been telling me if anyone here is interested in a JK themed version of "Truth or Bullshit" for good fun (or maybe helpful to some)

### JanSz — 2019-06-13T23:57:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-272608>

I would love to read what you will post.

My DW had a lung cancer that metastasized to her spine.
She found about all that when one of her vertebra collapsed 50%.

.

### caroline — 2019-06-14T03:17:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-272613>

Hi Vervaina .....so glad to see you here. I have thought of you so often and wondered how you are doing.

Please share what you are learning.....

How are you doing?

### Jenelle — 2019-06-14T12:53:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-272621>

I have thought of you often, as well. So good to hear from you!

### Phosphene — 2019-06-14T19:31:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-272647>

Thinking of you as well. Please share!

### Vervaina — 2019-06-20T01:24:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-272986>

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) I was sorry to read about your DW last year. I imagine you have a lot of acquired info on this subject.

### Vervaina — 2019-06-20T01:27:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-272987>

[@Phosphene](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19648/) [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) [@Jenelle](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18778/)

Thank you for your kindness. I am well and glad to be posting today

### Vervaina — 2019-06-20T01:29:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-272988>

It is interesting that when you have a proliferation of cancer cells you are no longer able to enjoy many of the lifestyle and habits that provide “health and wellness” to the average person. I guess this is because the cancer cells are our very own cells and not some foreign invaders we are trying to kill.

So what makes YOU strong and healthy can often also strengeth YOUR cancer cells as well. Likewise some of the things that are bad for you (and particularly your mitochondria and microbiome) will often be bad for your cancer cells as well. Chemo is an example of something hard on both you and your cancer cells, so is becoming mildly anemic. Glutathione is an example of something that is good for our bodies but can also bring benefit to our cancer cells making them stronger and harder to kill.

The point is that striving for health and wellness is not always serving you when you are trying to save your life because you have cancer. Once in remission you can then try and repair all the damage that you taken by weakening and killing (or healing depending on your point of view) your cancer cells bringing your body back into a state of redox, balance, and health.

I have found it so interesting to think along this new paradigm – that something that is “ good “for me isn’t always what I should be doing right now. And that what is “bad” for me may very well be what I SHOULD be doing right now.

### Vervaina — 2019-06-20T01:29:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-272989>

But I wonder about the whole picture - of course some of the things we do to treat cancer can cause cancer (both conventional and some alternative treatments can do this). Even some of the natural therapies meant to be hard on cancer can still raise stress hormones like cortisol and increase things like COX2 or VEGF which can benefit the cancer. So was the treatment then worth it in the end? Hard to know.

But grounding, UV, hydration, and sleep… the fundamental given to us by nature are all safe and mostly cost free ways to benefit your body without benefiting the cancer (well safe UV exposure anyway but see below). ​

### Vervaina — 2019-06-20T01:29:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-272990>

Ok so here is one… does this sound probable or like BS?

One of my doctors that is pro UV says that UV is not so straightforward for cancer patients. Many cancer patients have a mutated P53 gene (as I do) that isn’t working. P53 plays an important role in apoptosis, DNA repair and stability.

P53 is stimulated by UV light but if the gene is broken then UV isn’t turning it on (or at least not turning it on enough).

So here is the scenario they have found - The person with cancer goes out in the UV and P53 isn’t working like it should be so they are not protected from the UV. Apoptosis isn’t stimulated and any DNA damage from the radiation isn’t being repaired.

So basically if you have a P53 gene that isn’t working and you already have cancer you therefore should already have the perfect internal terrain for growing more cancer (like a Warburg shifted metabolism, high lactate levels, sticky blood, low functioning immune system, etc). Then could the sun be dangerous in this situation? Is getting as much UV as you can a bad idea if you have cancer?

One more finding is that sunlight is mutagenic enough to continue to worsen the mutation of the P53 gene when it is not working to protect us (and itself)

But I am sure this is not the whole story. I have read that research has found that the highest number of cancer patients effected by this situation are those who live in higher elevations. And since P53 is connected to O2, CO2, and oxidative stress there is more going on here than UV radiation.

One could try there luck and get so much UV it might fix there P53 mutation which may eliminate the cancer. But AM sunlight sounds like it would be a safer option here

SO could this be a situation of sunlight being good for our wellness unless one has cancer/P53 mutation?

### caroline — 2019-06-20T02:38:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-272991>

I wish I could help in some way Vervaina ......

But .....I can listen - and think and pray positive thoughts for you xoxo

### Swen — 2019-08-26T04:26:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-276635>

I am led to think that autophagy and lowering deuterium levels would create conditions that would never give the gene a choice in the matter of cancer. Dr Laszlo Boros reversed cancer pre-cursors by lowering his deuterium levels and intake via plenty of fatty meats and a bit of ddw. I don't remember him mentioning the role of sunlight or other kruse protocols with these steps; which is a question I would like to pose to him that might already be answered in Kruse's private thoughts and writings on deuterium.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-08-26T16:38:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-276661>

I don't think DDW saved him.........moving out of LA for 6 months a year however was more wise.

### JanSz — 2019-08-26T17:01:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-girl-who-lived.23823/post-276663>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=276661)
>
> I don't think DDW saved him.........moving out of LA for 6 months a year however was more wise.

DDW=Deuterium Depleted Water
Boros hardly drinks any water. He admits to drinking (0.2 - 0.25) liters/day
When comparing that to 5-6 liters/day (that you support), it would be immaterial if his water must be DDW.
But he may be worrying about the deuterium that he breads in.
In some places, I have seen speculations that air-conditioned air is less humid. So it contains less water. We are breathing about 2 liters of water daily.
Overall, he is a gong ho, on reducing deuterium intake any way he can, saying that any drinkable water should have no more than 130ppm (D/H).
He looks good and is active, physically, and mentally.
I assume that he likely pees out much more than hi drinks.
What he does doesn't dehydrate him. (I would take that as a given).
His explanation is based on not getting dehydrated by doing what he does.

It would not hurt to check osmolality, but when one would exclude peoples with weak kidneys, then it seems that his method may have some validity.

....
...
