### wildperoxin — 2019-07-18T19:43:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sincerest-greetings.23953/post-274544>

Hi all,

First off, I could be better at introductions, and I’m nervous as all get-out. There’s too much I want to put down here.

I have no known severe or chronic health condition per se, unless we’re counting anaemia and allergens. I have observable sensitivity to environmental factors. Perhaps more sensitive than I can fully be conscious of and I lie to myself/justify that I’m stepping into hypochondriac extremes. By and large, I’m more upset or concerned when seeing others suffer, and by lies/lack of clarity and transparency i.e. their health deteriorates, psychological and other forms of abuse, etc.

Mostly, I want to network. I need help, to understand things better, and to get things done, as well as improve production capabilities.

Living in the UK with a single parent and three younger adolescent siblings is like fighting a maelstrom while climbing a mountain sometimes. We are first generation. Part of the reason I remained with my family or in the country was in the hope that I was able to set up a better living space and support while here, either provide information when they ask re: light, water, magnetism i.e. setup Ethernet/internet of things, lighting, water source, and so on. If I were to move. Say, yes, able to acquire some of these things e.g. a water distiller and alternative lighting/blackout options, yet still ways to go.

The city we live in has recently rolled out 5G. Preparations to pack and go, at least temporarily, for me have come to mind. I receive some support from my family, and I seem to have this belief that nowhere else will provide that. I know logically and feel intuitively that there’s better support. Blood is hard to leave.

Back to the point of first generation: we are originally from East Africa, not far from the equator. I was born there. I have more family there, and I am and have been training, studying and working so that I can practice and work back home.

When I was younger, I made a solo trip back to our home country. Holy cow – talk about a world of difference in terms of all areas of health! Mood, energy levels and so on improved. There’s a link with being closer to the equator, or maybe I feel better in my country of origin.

There’s much empirical evidence of people travelling from higher latitudes down closer to the equator and feeling better, typically after a period of acclimatization.

So, I’d like to ask for help. What actions or suggestions with regards to family?

I’m really looking forward to exploring these forums more and engaging with people. Due to shyness, or uncertainties and anxieties, I realised how important it was to join y’all here, and did not before. That’s changing. Thank you all for sharing your wonderful experiences over the years! And thank you Dr. Kruse et. al.

‘till next time,

Best!

### wildperoxin — 2019-07-22T16:30:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sincerest-greetings.23953/post-274756>

Thanks everyone! And yes to the Optimal Journal!

[@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) I'm working on becoming a practitioner, and I had hoped to do clinical research also. I studied Physics at university, opted for Mathematical Physics, took some time off to work and study in Health and Social Care and Health and Social Sciences; I started doing Complementary Healthcare, and now I don't know if I want to stay in the UK to finish. I'm a qualified practitioner in some \[CAM\] therapies, still training in others. I've thought that it might be a better approach to improve my Spanish and go continue to study somewhere else, or stick solely to distant learning course structures?

Education's been a rough ride. One thing I found tricky was being in rooms with artificial lights and nnEMFs for extended periods, and living in university halls of residence for the first 2 years - that was a bad choice. I work with patients who have Alzheimer's and dementia, among other conditions. It is so extremely upsetting and infuriating at times seeing how mainstream healthcare can push their backwardly, upside down standards to and for insane ends and their effects on a general populace.

I have blue-light blocking glasses which are obviously orange/red tinted. Funnily: one other Physics student at our university used to wear these orange-tinted glasses all the time - indoors or outdoors. I want to get some clear ones that I can wear while working, because sometimes you need to see what you're doing clearly

I'm in a situation of financial uncertainty/instability, otherwise, I'd pack up and move right away! Or at least travel to a place with lower population density, nnEMFs and artifical lights and where I can increase my solar callus.

Cold thermogenesis help me quit coffee/caffeine, of which I'm super grateful for. We live in an area near a river with a lot of woodland, another thing to appreciate. The amount of UVA and UVB here at a relatively lower altitude of ~39ft is not optimal. UVA lamps help; albeit bulbs need replacing. In the evenings, red LED lamp indoor, or/then straight up candles. I have an IR lamp with visible red light that needs its bulb replaced. Just for some context, as there are lots of other details. When 5G rolled out a few months back, I was like, "Well, ya knew it was coming! You didn't know when but here it is!"

I'm going to Spain for a week soon, 40° latitude, and hoping the seafood will be good!

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=274599)
>
> [View attachment 12648](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/12648/)

100%.

### JanSz — 2019-07-27T15:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sincerest-greetings.23953/post-275054>

[@WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/)
[@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)
or anybody else
The average water contains 150 deuterium atoms per million hydrogens.
150/1000000=0.015 %

on post #25 or #26 above
dr Boros uses 0.015 % as an approximation of the volume of heavy water within all water.
So 0.015% of 7.2 cubic meters is
0.00015\*7200=1.08 liters
Is that correct?
It would be correct if all deuterium containing water was DHO.
But there is D2O.
If all deuterium containing water was D2O the 1.08 would be halved.

**What is the ratio of D2O, HDO, and H2O ?**
\---------------
a(16)+b(17)+c(18) = weight
a+b+c = 1L
\---------------

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-07-22T18:42:00+0300

> [Ossa said:](/goto/post?id=274571)
>
> How is it that microwaves dehydrate us but radiation with higher frequency does not?

There is much to learn about the pathology of microwave radiation sickness (virtually all of us have it to some degree unless you have been living under a rock).

Heating of tissue is a minor concern, IMO.

**One of the most fascinating side effects of RF/MW radiation** we experience everyday from insidious electrosmog that blankets our planet is interference with body's innate cell signalling communication system.

**Our bodies have an amazing quantum physics-like system of microtubules** that connect cells and connective tissue. These minute tubes (a huge number for each cell) connect cells through gap junctions in cell walls.

As I understand it, **cells transmit messages using photons through these tubules at specific body frequencies.** Microwaves disturbs these transmissions and induce their own, completely alien frequency into the process, screwing it up.

Of course there are many other deleterious effects of microwaves at play, especially disturbances of **voltage-gated calcium channels** in cells.

The more you learn about the evils of RF/MW the more you wonder about sanity of mankind, and whether we are fit to survive on this planet.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-07-22T21:47:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-physics-of-microwaves.23954/post-274790>

The major effect of microwaves is felt on covalent bonds, their bonding angles, and as a result, this changes their molecular resonance.

I did one some time ago, results apparently "moderate", not optimal,

Extra-cellular Water: 13.3

Intra-cellular Water: 22.9 - 30.5 Liters
Extra-cellular Water: 15 - 22.4 Liters

"**The water you drink isn't the key.** **The water your mitochondria makes is**, and **the field that mitochondria are forced to persist in via your choices around EMFs is also germane to the story of water in your life."**
So I'm working on healthy lifestyle choices and choices around EMF's in my environment so my body can re-hydrate.

I've done what I learned to do to lower EMF's but now going to have an EMF evaluation of my home to understand what exposure I still have and will remediate if possible, as needful. We have been looking to move for some time as Dr. Kruse teaches that you never get well in the environment you got sick in. Living in the same home for the last 24 years and I am recovering from a health condition I had in 2009.
Until we can move I will spend more time outdoors grounding and I plan to purchase a EMF SHIELDING CANOPY to sleep under.

Dr. Kruse went on to explain...."When energy (an EMF) is absorbed from the field/environment, particles of matter will begin to oscillate between two configurations, the ground state, and an excited state. In particular, all particles in water coupled to the same wavelength of the fluctuations will oscillate in phase with the EM field of Earth and begin to exhibit quantum coherent behavior in all the water on Earth. This organizing principle is how the Schumann resonance made water on Earth quantum coherent before any life was present on Earth. This is why every living thing has an alpha wave of 7.83 Hz. How is this possible? Understanding the Lamb shift and what it does was key to making sense of why the Schumann resonance makes life possible on Earth because the Schumann plus water was the blueprint 4.6 billion years ago for mitochondria. It took life 3.95 billion years to get the creation of mitochondria right to match what water and the Schumann resonance showed at Earth's genesis."

# Thread: Allison’s OJ to “Fabulous 40” & beyond...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allison%E2%80%99s-oj-to-%E2%80%9Cfabulous-40%E2%80%9D-beyond.23960/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### *~Allison~* — 2019-07-20T21:49:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allison%E2%80%99s-oj-to-%E2%80%9Cfabulous-40%E2%80%9D-beyond.23960/post-274661>

I turn 40 next summer. Yippee! Woo Hoo! It is going to be fabulous. As an old soul I’ve always liked getting older. Really. Even as a child I was often confused WHY people seemed to resist aging. Baffles me still, although I do get the rationale of wanting to hold on to what’s familiar. I’ve often joked that getting older is better than the alternative. People laugh and ask, what’s the alternative? I say ... dying lol. Most balk, of course. I’m ornery - teehee. Anyhow, I feel like my age is finally catching up with my inside perspective. Sometimes I look down at my hands and it feels like Quantum Leep - I’m like, whoa my hands look really young! Lol So I truly believe I’m in for a long & prosperous life, but man it’s been hard. And it seems it’s going to get harder. When I look around at folks, what Dr Kruse is saying makes complete sense ... not everyone is going to be able to “make it” through this extinction event. I’m going to keep proceeding as if I’m going to be one of them. Every day builds my resume. There’s ALWAYS hope.

So ... I’ll start my journal with my mom - God’love’r. I’m so hopeful that Dr Kruse says to look at your mother. (Yay! Good job mom!) I despised her in my youth, but in hindsight she’s the greatest inspiration I have in accomplishing my health goals. As a God-fearing woman for much of her life, she has depended on God & Nature out of pure instinct; it’s her way of life. That’s why I’m SO AT HOME in the Kruse message. I’m like - YES! - finally a doctor that’s preaching like my mother has preached my entire life lol And my mom has been right soooo many times in my life. Good thing I finally started listening.

She’s 78 & lives in the large family home she (nearly) raised 5 children in. She married shortly after high school, had 4 children, then divorced & married my dad, had me, then divorced again. Since the early 80’s she’s been a single mom & taken care of the house with only the occasional help. (Some jobs just require the strength of a brute ) She’s not obese, but she’s just slightly chubby in her waist. Her arms and legs are well proportioned and she’s always had the curves Dr Kruse speaks about as beneficial for genetic lineage. So do me and my two (half) sisters.

My mom has never “doctored.” One of her retirement requirements was to get a Physician & get a physical. She passed everything perfectly. She figured out a way to retire early (55) because she can live on very little money. After my dad died in 2016 she thought it would be wise to see if she still had a doctor because it had been over a decade since her first physical. Good thing she checked because the doctor was gone lol She had to get a new PCP; and again passed all her tests perfectly. God’love’r. I don’t think she’s ever taken an antibiotic? Definitely never gets a flu shot and has never taken a long-term RX. She’ll take a Valium before going to the dentist and pain reliever post dental work, but never the whole bottle. The only thing she takes regularly? ... is an aspirin about 3X a week. She almost made it with a near clean slate. You can see why she’s such an inspiration to me. ✨☺️✨She doesn’t even take a multivitamin or ever stepped foot in a health food shop.

All of my moms children have never been obese. We’ve all had our - overweight stage - but never chronically, nor excessive. To this day, all 5 of us are decent weight for our height & age. My (half) brothers are in generally good health, or I never hear of any issues. My one sister left my mom for my Aunts house in high school; she didn’t take the divorce very well, so I understand. But she missed out on a lot of my moms preaching and ended up with osteoporosis in her mid-20’s. Dr Kruse chapter on osteoporosis was VERY interesting to me. My sister has been bound to the nursing home for last 5 years+ (?) and prays for the day she’ll pass and be reunited with her son. It’s very sad. My other sister has been in good health until recently when she got Hashimoto’s & then removed her thyroid. (She listened to my mom’s preaching but was likely influenced by her career as an RN). Her daughter, my niece like a sister, also removed part of her thyroid due to a nodule.

My dad was a hippie. A REAL hippie lol He used to lecture me on what it REALLY meant. Not the typical associations people think of, but the social-economic-religious-type movement that swept the young people of the nation. He made a consorted effort to make sure hippies got a good name. I think for the most part, they did. But he was really hard on his body, playing in bands, hitch hiking across the country, drinking, smoking, partying ... and many night shift jobs. He almost ALWAYS stayed up late and slept through the day. He ate SAD or worse, but never overweight. Well, once he had a fat stage, but lost it all. He hated going barefoot & didn’t use sunscreen because he just avoided the sun all together. I was so mad at him when I was a child, because when he took me to the pool we had to sit in the shade lol He believed that nature was “the answer” though. He was an artist in touch with the beauty of the world. While he fell victim to a low income lifestyle, and the medical system with all the Rx, radiating tests, creams, shots, devices, and lord knows what else ... they seemed to exacerbate the underlying issues. He actually was in excellent health most of his life. It was after the awful car accident in 1999 that things went downhill fast. He didn’t have the financial means to pursue a natural health plan - although he most certainly would have. It’s disappointing as a loving daughter to see your dad slave to a system that contributed to his demise. In my opinion. I paid to take him to some natural doctors, but was too little too late. He lived a full life though. God’love’em ; )

So back another generation...

My dad’s dad lived until 88 and basically died from a brain aneurysm & had heart issues; my dad’s mom died at age 94 from Alzheimer’s & had heart issues. My mom’s dad died at 62 (before I was born) due to a hemorrhage caused by Alcoholism; and my mom’s mom died at age 97 due to breast cancer & had heart issues.

As I study doctor Kruse’s work it’s like adding hindsight filters to my life. The environmental factors are quite obvious in turning disease on in my family lineage. I have great examples of what to do, and what not to do. I wholly admire that my mom has withstood peer pressure and more mocking than any person should have in order to be in touch with her true self. She’s unwavering in her own path. Truly amazing.

Can you believe she can STILL see far away? I was shocked when I found this out the other day when telling her about Dr Kruse. I was like - Whoa. My myopia started my senior year of high school.

So I’m sick ... but I’m determined to reverse my disease. I love studying miracles and the people who don’t get sick. I KNOW like I KNOW like I KNOW, that my condition can be reversed.

What do I have? ... Lord only knows lol Actually, I can - NOW - confidently conclude that I have a mitochondrial disease. (TY Dr Kruse! TY for giving it a name lol) I’ve spent thousands of dollars over the years trying to figure out “what’s wrong” with me until finally I said - I don’t care what it is, I just need to heal myself. I knew something was wrong!

I have lots of symptoms and no diagnosis. Well, the only true diagnosis I have is MTHFR and hardly anyone even knows what that is lol. I’ve worked at a mega-corporation for 10 years behind dual monitors and many of those years were under an extreme amount of OT. Last summer I took a 13 week (paid) leave of absence from work to heal. (Btw I’m back on leave right now) After my dad died in 2016 my TSH had started to climb. My previous PCP wanted me to go on RX for THE REST OF MY LIFE. When I didn’t take the first one, she said because I was into natural stuff she’d then prescribe the medication that was derived from pigs. (LMAO; she tried) So I got a new doctor. This time I went Integrated.

I’ve only seen my new Integrated PCP twice last year and once this year. I do most of my doctoring with naturopaths and chiropractors. My new PCP is soooooo awesome though, I absolutely love her. She looked at my thermograms, my bio-meridian scan, and my blue blockers and knew all about all of them. And she suggested a GI MAP Stool Test. I’m so excited to see what’s in my shit! Lol No one could explain WHY my TSH was high until ... DR Kruse. My mouth literally dropped when I read that 20% of the inactive T3 is converted to the active T4 in the gut. In my work, 20% is the threshold we use for a significant amount. So my brain thinks we need to stimulate more hormones but really it’s probably not being converted due to my long term gut issues. This explains why my thyroid looks great on my thermogram and bio-meridian scan, but my gut is troubled. What. A. Relief. Finally! Some clarity! I’m just beside myself in appreciation for this knowledge.

Before I get too far ... last year when I was on LOA I got my TSH from nearly 6.0 down to 1.96. IDK which of the million things I was doing healed me, I just know my labs reversed. I went back to work 09/2018 and by 06/2019 it’s back up to 4.47. So yes, I’m allergic to work - my teammates said. Funny, but true. Sigh.

So here I am. On leave until probably October. This time I’ll know exactly how and why I healed myself ... and will maintain it this time. = P I’ve seen the sunrise everyday except two, and most sunsets. I’ve always been a great sleeper & dreamer. I’m eating Paleo. Going to the farmers market. I have blue blockers I wear pridefully. We have blueblocker screen protectors on our phones. Orange & red lights. Using candles. Being outside; barefoot. I stopped wearing sunglasses. I have two Knicki swimsuits ... and the best for last ... woo wee I’m going hard on the CT! Yeah baby! Many of my symptoms are pain & inflammation related to musculoskeletal issues. I’m currently in the 2nd round of an intensive corrective chiropractor program. Results are promising.

I have a handful of questions I’m gathering, but one is VERY pressing. I was a bad mom yesterday = ( I didn’t put enough sunblock on my 6 and 11 year old red heads and one got burnt to a crisp. I feel horrible. I got burnt too! I just totally under estimated it all. Long story but I’m already onto my serenity prayer. My son couldn’t sleep much last night it hurt so bad & has had real tears. (I’d like to interject here: that kids these days are just a little too fluffy, in my opinion. I was so much tougher than him lol) He started out with his T-shirt on and so I didn’t get sunblock on his body at all. What’s the best long term game plan to get off of sunblock anyhow? What SPF? Should I go down in SPF or keep the higher SPF & change frequency? Is there a thread on this somewhere? I don’t want to use it anymore, but I also need to do this in (better) stages. I’ve bought many natural sunblocks but they don’t seem to work well, ugh.

Also, both the kids have been going to the sunrise with me on occasion. I’m curious if the biological process that’s turned on at sunrise that later protects us in the more intense sun, is on a day-to-day basis? Or do you have to see the sunrise cumulatively in order to have that biological sun protection? Does that make sense? I mean, the two days I missed the sunrise, I completely avoided the sun those days. Especially the mid-day sun. I felt like I’d be more vulnerable. But since I’m going to see the sunrise cumulatively, could I still feel safe out in the mid-day sun on a day I missed the sunrise?

Thanks for having me here! I appreciate in advance any advice you want to share. I’d love to hear how you came off sunblock. I’ve learned a lot, but I still have so much more to learn.

P.S. FYI I’m not on my phone very much so I may be delayed in my responses. I avoid technology as much as possible. Btw I’m so grateful for this forum because I avoid Facebook as much as possible too. = )

### Sue-UK — 2019-07-21T12:49:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-sun.23962/post-274705>

Hi Nichole

In the natural environment of the lake, you can still ground on the rocks. The sphinx pose isn't mandatory, particularly if there are better n=1 options available, or safer ones. If you can safely stand barefoot in the water at the lake, that would be a good option. Or if you can't do that, standing on, or sitting barefoot on the rocks, and/or grounding via your hands, it all counts. You can still ground sat on your butt, if you have bare parts of you connected to the earth/rocks. If your butt is bare, that's a source of grounding.

The girl in the picture is dressed. Naked would be better, but if she lifted the bottom of her top up so that her belly was exposed, on bare rock or earth she would be doing some direct belly grounding, as well as grounding through the top of her feet and her hands and up to her elbows. It may vary when and where you do the grounding, and how you are dressed, but the trick is to get the most bare surface (feet, hands, body) **_safely_** connected to the earth, or exposed to the sun, without getting arrested.

Do you receive a benefit in winter and fall in Chicago? As opposed to sitting indoors under junk lighting, yes.

Is it enough solar power to move the needle, less likely.

Winter vacations to the equator may be useful for noticing your body's response to higher solar power.

### nichole — 2019-07-25T23:34:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pm-lighting.23968/post-275001>

> [Joce\_ said:](/goto/post?id=274931)

### Ed Pomicter — 2019-07-26T00:02:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pm-lighting.23968/post-275003>

> [nichole said:](/goto/post?id=275000)
>
> Is that a wick candle ?
>
> How many of those do you use at night?

It is a kerosene lamp. Growing up my family had a weekend home with no electricity so I am very used to this light. We now live in an area with a large old order Mennonite community, so there is actually a kerosene lamp store. That one is from the 1880s I believe. I would not have much fear about using these with kids around; my brother and I were less than 5 when we started using them and we just were taught to respect that they are hot and they are a flame. Kerosene, like diesel, is not explosive and hard to burn if you were to spill it and then throw a match into it.

We tend to not use many at night at this time of year because we go to bed when (or before) twilight falls. We use a couple in the morning in the kitchen when we are preparing breakfast to watch the sunrise. We have 5 for if we want to light things up. You can use regular kerosene which has a bit of a petroleum smell especially in a tight house, or super refined kerosene ("heater kerosene" at our hardware store) which has essentially no smell.

### Saichi — 2019-07-26T00:25:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pm-lighting.23968/post-275005>

Ed you're not concerned about breathing petrochemicals?

# Thread: Music and Messages?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/music-and-messages.23969/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### 5G Canary — 2019-07-21T15:19:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/music-and-messages.23969/post-274715>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I stumbled upon an article about a recently released track of Freddy Mercury. It was called... Freddy’s lost vocal performance. This unreleased last and only solo vocal appearance by Freddy gave me chills. Freddy did this track completely solo with only a piano.

Freddy didn’t want the solo version - he really wanted his band mates in it and filled with a togetherness energy.... that was the version that was released in 1986. The togetherness would make sense at the time. The words were/are definitely a precursor to the future at the time. However, the insightful words weren’t written by Freddy... it appears like it was a message straight from Mother Nature maybe to Freddy at the time?

Maybe this 2019 new recently released “lost solo” is to us now? Done as a solo is significant to our current times. Using his brilliance as a vessel... Maybe? Freddy singing/performing.... His passion oozed into everything he did. This solo performance seems to scream a message from heaven...... it **awed** me!

A quote I liked from the article...

“We all know how good Freddy is. But this shows how extra special he is when nobody knows and there’s nobody else accompanying him.”

“We might as well be deaf and dumb and blind.... I know that sounds unkind

But it seems to me we've not listened to..... Or spoken about it at all

The fact that time is running out for us all
Time waits for nobody
Time waits for no-one
We've got to build this world together
Or we'll have no more future at all
Because time - it waits for no-one

Let's learn to be friends with one another
Or there'll be no more future at all......

### Lahelada — 2019-07-21T18:02:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/music-and-messages.23969/post-274722>

I love this. Thank you for sharing !

Hi all,

I was listening to this interview:

...right at the end Jack puts exercise on the bottom of the list of priorities:

Circadian Rhythm
DHA
Limiting blue light
Water (spring water)
Sleep
Cold
Psychological stress
Exercise

In listening to an interview with Dr. Gerald Pollack, Gerald mentions that positive charge is excreted through sweat and also mentions that pressure on water has positive effects. It would seem exercise encourages both, the excretion of positive charge and also builds pressure in the muscles of the body which would transfer into water in the body and cells.

Any thoughts?

### kris90 — 2019-07-23T20:10:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise.23976/post-274881>

It's because exercise should only be focused on once leptin sensitivity occurs. If we cannot uncouple (which requires leptin sensitivity), we cannot release heat from the mitochondria and we produce a ton of ROS from exercise. It actually increases inflammation, worsens the leptin resistant state and shortens telomeres.

Once leptin sensitive, exercise can have great benefits if done in moderation. It can help generate the right ROS signals for mitochondrial biogenesis as mentioned above.

# Thread: DOES LOWERED TESTOSTERONE LEAD TO POOR COGNITIVE FUNCTION?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-lowered-testosterone-lead-to-poor-cognitive-function.23980/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2019-07-23T18:29:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-lowered-testosterone-lead-to-poor-cognitive-function.23980/post-274869>

Short answer is YES.

**When to Screen, When to Treat injuries in traumatic brain injury cases?**
Anything that alters the sex steroids cascade can lead to chronic brain injury. This can be from drugs, supplements, a lack of sun, or trauma. Is this why testosterone lowering drugs can be a problem in those with TBI risks? YES.
Is this why playing professional sports comes with serious long term risks for developing hormone and brain dysfunction? Yes. Can this effect be seen in kids too? Yes.
Head TRAUMA is a big deal in humans.
There is no debate that TBI-related endocrine dysfunction is a widely missed diagnosis. The healthcare providers who are first to treat these injuries are often unaware of potential long-term effects.
Emergency room staff \[and primary care doctors\] are the ones seeing the kids come in with the head injuries and they’re thinking concussion and all the old school stuff about concussion-causing headaches, maybe some dizziness, and vomiting. The common perception in thinking with TBI, is that the kids are going to get better on their own with time, and they’re just not aware that you can have these hormonal changes associated with the TBI that slow recovery or lead to a non recovery longer term and set up the risk for neurodegeneration and dementia or ALS.
Diagnosing post-TBI dysfunction involves serum screening tests including 8:00 AM cortisol levels, midnight melatonin levels, thyroid-stimulating hormone, luteinizing hormone, follicle-stimulating hormone, IGF, free thyroxine, testosterone for male patients, and estradiol for females. However, there have been several published guidelines on the recommended screening intervals and even treatment.
There are a lot of transient deficiencies in the first year that makes it difficult to base lifelong treatment on early results. The best screening is a careful history of symptoms of adrenal, thyroid, or gonadotropin deficiency or much more importantly, the quantitation of linear growth in kids or in lowered IGF-1 levels for adults. TBI causes both acute and chronic pregnenolone steal syndrome and is usually linked to low Vitamin D 25 (OH) levels and higher LDL cholesterol.
[https://medicalxpress.com/news/2019-07-dementia-tied-hormone-blocking-prostate-cancer.html](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2019-07-dementia-tied-hormone-blocking-prostate-cancer.html)

### kris90 — 2019-07-25T14:25:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-lowered-testosterone-lead-to-poor-cognitive-function.23980/post-274981>

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) I highly recommend reading the CT 6 blog, it will be helpful for you to understand.

My lifestyle pre-Jack made me hypogonad: exercising 5-6 days a week under blue light, calorie restriction and IFing while leptin resistant/adrenal fatigue. The missing link was becoming cold adapted. I fixed the hypogonadism without changing my levels (actually levels slightly decreased) but I allowed my receptors to become sensitized. If I stayed warm adapted, I would have to eat a boatload of carbs (i.e. my Ray Peat experiment) to prevent hypogonadism. I tried that way, and it worked (tripled my T levels), but being cold adapted allows me to perform better in literally every way, and improve body composition.

When I talked to Jack in 2017 and re-read this from CT #6, I knew my situation was different than 99% of modern males:

> **So how does the Leptin receptor flip its function in cold doc?** Since cold acutely raises the serum leptin levels as it is liberated from fat, it confirmationally alters the leptin receptor to become more sensitive to the hormone level as it is liberate from the WAT. This process is a function of physics, (quantum) as all steroid receptors have higher affinities for their receptor molecule. This implies that even if a person has low hormone levels in a warm adapted state once they become cold adapted it does not matter!

### Beristere — 2021-12-02T00:08:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-lowered-testosterone-lead-to-poor-cognitive-function.23980/post-306929>

Among older men with decreased testosterone levels and age-related memory changes, the use of testosterone medication for one year does not improve memory and other cognitive functions compared to placebo. I recommend using high-quality items like those provided by [rats.army](https://rats.army/). By the way, you may acquire a 190-page electronic handbook in this situation. You'll discover all the intricacies and nuances here, which was quite beneficial to me. For example, how to keep your health during the cycle while minimizing adverse effects. What is testosterone suppression, and how can it be avoided? How to interpret the results of a blood test. This, I feel, will be beneficial to you.

### Brando — 2024-11-17T02:10:07+0200

The missing link was becoming cold adapted. I fixed the hypogonadism without changing my levels (actually levels slightly decreased) but I allowed my receptors to become sensitized. If I stayed warm adapted, I would have to eat a boatload of carbs (i.e. my Ray Peat experiment) to prevent hypogonadism. I tried that way, and it worked (tripled my T levels), but being cold adapted allows me to perform better in literally every way, and improve body composition.

### kris90 — 2024-11-19T21:25:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-lowered-testosterone-lead-to-poor-cognitive-function.23980/post-342653>

> [Brando said:](/goto/post?id=342461)
>
> How long until you started to see results?

It was fast and furious once everything was fully implemented. I moved out of the city in Spring 2016 and it was my reset: woke up to watch sunrise, no blue light at night, grounding and CT. I was still on HCG monotherapy at this time, but from that alone, (same dosing on HCG), my T levels shot up from midrange to just over the range. This happened over about a 2 month period, but within a week or 2, I already felt big improvements in wellbeing.

Fast forward to Fall and entering Winter. Same routine, but way more CT, and strict ketogenic diet with seafood. Came off the HCG, and the first time I didn't experience a crash, so I continued on. Got bloodwork in early 2017 and was shocked at how low my T went, but how amazing I felt. My SHBG went through the roof too, and I later realised SHBG transports testosterone to androgen receptors, and is not always the "bad guy". I was curious what was going on, so I stumbled upon Cold Thermogenesis 6, and everything clicked. Then I attended Nourish Vermont that Spring and spoke to Jack about it, and he confirmed I was living proof of the "ancient pathway".

Takehome: connect with nature and adapt to the seasons, your body will take care of the rest. Hope this helps.

### kris90 — 2024-11-20T16:14:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-lowered-testosterone-lead-to-poor-cognitive-function.23980/post-342689>

To add further to this story, in March 2017 I decided to implement a reverse bio-hack after spending some time reading about Ray Peat followers who increased their testosterone after coming off long-term low carb diets. I wanted to explore this, so I stopped all CT, took hot showers, dressed warm, stopped eating seafood (low PUFA), significantly increased carbs to around 300g per day from honey, orange juice, rice and potatoes and increased saturated fat intake mostly from butter and coconut oil. After a month of this, I got bloods drawn, and my testosterone tripled and SHBG dropped. I gained a little bit of fat, and noticed my hunger signals were out of whack. I couldn't go long periods without food whereas previously, I could do OMAD with no issues.

This experiment allowed me to observe and experience the difference between warm adapted physiology versus cold adapted, and I realised living in Canada and embracing the high latitude living was clearly the way to go since I did not have the UV to offset the carbload and artificially warm environment I created. Experiencing minimal hunger, a great sense of wellbeing, and superhuman strength and energy like the sherpas was a lot more convenient. So I went back to this way of living, and sure enough within a couple of months, my testosterone levels dropped back down to low-mid range, and I felt better metabolically speaking.

# Thread: ANTIAGING MEME'S AND FALLACIES
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antiaging-memes-and-fallacies.23981/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2019-07-23T19:43:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antiaging-memes-and-fallacies.23981/post-274878>

**ANTIAGING MEME'S TO AVOID**

This post is loaded with so many anti-aging marketing meme’s it is sickening. Let take apart one, #7. I am a neurosurgeon so protein misfolding is an expertise of mine. Most people are unaware that we can monitor protein misfolding dynamics because of the hydrogen/deuterium exchange process. This can be done via mass spectrometry (HDX-MS). HDX-MS has emerged as a powerful method for characterizing protein conformational dynamics in cells. The basis of this method is the fact that backbone amides in stable hydrogen-bonded structures (e.g., α-helices and β-sheets) are protected against exchange with the aqueous solvent. All protein structures in humans are hydrated with some form of water with a varying amount of Hydrogen to deuterium mix based upon its location in the cell. All protein structures become dynamic because of this arrangement because of information protons bring or subtract from the proteins in question. These protons are created when light hits the hydrogen bonding network in water to exclude protons. This is how light acts as an information processor in cells. This even plays a massive role in DNA/RNA helices. It is also clear from these protein studies, however, that eventually all of the protecting hydrogen bonds will transiently breakdown as the protein — according to quantum thermodynamic principles — via cycles that control folding.

The redox power (light) in the water surrounding proteins controls this process in protons. So if we change the fraction of H+/D around a protein we can see with mass spectrometry how protons impart information and energy to amino acids amine bonds by examining their partially unfolded states. These changes correspond to excited free energy and information levels. As a result, all of the backbone amides in biomolecules can and will eventually become temporarily solvent-exposed and exchange-competent over time as life is lived. The environment controls the fractionation of the surrounding water. This is how protein folding occurs in mast cell diseases and many other disorders of protein folding (AD, PD, ALS). Consequently, a folded protein in D2O will gradually incorporate deuterium into its backbone amides over H+, and the kinetics of the process can be readily monitored by mass spectrometry when we know where and what to look for. I expect this to be part of quantum medicine in the future. Nothing in this paper talks about this. This is a fluff marketing piece with science subtracted.

[https://medium.com/@nicktrehann/human-longevity-b3d622d7e658](https://medium.com/@nicktrehann/human-longevity-b3d622d7e658)

### John Schumacher — 2020-03-09T01:37:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antiaging-memes-and-fallacies.23981/post-287275>

Proteins either start folding as soon as they emerge from a ribosome as chains of amino-acid residues or require a helper molecule to fold into a well-defined three-dimensional structure, which is essential for their proper functioning. Misfolding is often a result of insufficient internal free energy.

My question: From an electromagnetizm perspective, would it be therapeutic to increase the electrical charge in the extracellular plasma? If so, what would a DC pulsed frequency (say 7.83 Hz) and voltage (say at least negative 50 milliwatts), delivered through myelin sheath conduits (creating completed electric circuits) while administrating IR light, provide to the electron transport (charged ions) in the extracellular plasma? Would it become "super charged", enhancing the "free" ions' potential for a greater therapeutic effect?

Most biological studies of proteins trek through the funnel of folding and unfolding only assume what occurred from point "A" to point "B" when analyzed under the organic-chemistry microscope. Specifically, how it is believed electrons (from helper molecules) might work (or move) from one chemical transformation to the next.

I have read "Light Shaping Life" and I'm astonished how little we know! Nature has so much to teach us. Are we ready (trainable) for her lessons?

# Thread: What else is birth control stealing from women?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-else-is-birth-control-stealing-from-women.23987/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LieselK — 2019-07-24T22:15:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-else-is-birth-control-stealing-from-women.23987/post-274950>

Her passion? Her purpose? Her amazing capabilities? Her power?

There are obvious issues to birth control, but a friend posed this to me yesterday...

Does the suppression of ovulation via birth control cause women to truly not desire to have babies? In which we lack natural cycling and foster contempt for the way a woman's body was created and the job it was created to be able to do. In contrast, when we embrace cycling and our created purpose we honor the power of the woman's body to be fertile, grow life and birth it.

Whether we choose to do it or not should truly be a choice. Are we robbing ourselves of the choice by altering the natural cycle? Not just because we can't ovulate with birth control, but because we can't even feel what we're supposed to feel about being a woman anymore? Is it making women numb?

I ask because, like my friend who posed this question, I see many women who "choose" birth control because they are afraid. Afraid of getting pregnant, sure... but why? Afraid of feeling. Afraid of wanting. Afraid of being judged for wanting. Afraid of being a woman who cycles. It seems easier for them to take birth control and hold onto their contempt than to wrestle with the innate feelings of wanting something so badly even if the right choice for them is to not have that baby.

I can ask these questions because I wrestle. If I really had a choice to be numb to it, would I choose it? I don't think so. I've put so much effort into being able to feel alive.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-07-24T22:47:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-else-is-birth-control-stealing-from-women.23987/post-274953>

I personally think it is a major effect.

Sex and reproduction are electromagnetic phenomena so anything that interrupts it by any means creates collateral effects.

### Richelle Jones — 2019-07-25T07:25:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-else-is-birth-control-stealing-from-women.23987/post-274975>

i read somewhere that BCP change your sense of smell and smell has a big role in selecting partners with good and different immune systems to our own which is necessary for strong and adaptable offspring. When your are on BCP you are more likely to select someone with immune system similar to yours = more like a family member....so if you choose your childrens father when you are on BCP dont be surprised if you dont really like him after you go off the pill. Might explain my first two marriages....

# Thread: Breathing life into a dead thread

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2019-07-25T21:48:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breathing-life-into-a-dead-thread.23991/post-274998>

A cravat not an excuse...I am new, learning my way around.
An _Egoscue Jack Kruse_ search led me to this thread from "Just Breathe"
I had **3+ comments ready to share** but ...should have read page 2 first as that thread was closed due to avatar infraction.
_**[Look back and face forward | Jack Kruse Optimal Health Forum](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/)**_
_forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/
[Jan 24, 2017 - She turned me on to the Egoscue method. It consists of a series of exercises that take a half hour. They put your spine into realignment.](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/)_
3 of m[y Comments](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/)
\[FONT=Trebuchet MS, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif\][1 Egoscue (Pete Egoscue the Founder of the Egoscue Method) has greatly helped me with flexibility and postural alignment. I do a 20 min routine _Pain Free Performance on Berkmar in Charlottesville VA_ prescribed. Their charge was out of pocket... but I later found a Physical Therapist trained in Egoscue who worked with me who did take insurance so if your insurance allows and you find the right practitioner that would help defray the cost. Even the out of pocket cost reasonable for the value. I have never taken any muscular skeletal pain to them that they have not fixed and once in one session they eliminated a bad lingering dizzy spell. I have never left their studio in pain. I don't have to keep going back if I do my routine on a regular basis. If I miss my 20 min routine I at least do their Static Back exercise morning and night. Feels like doing nothing ...but it does! Free and Easy.
Goggle or search on You Tube to find videos on Static Back.
Pete Egoscue is also the author of the book:
**Pain Free: A Revolutionary Method for Stopping Chronic Pain**
This book and the Zero Drop movement in the shoe and running industry influenced my neighbor against becoming a surgeon. Many believe and I agree, that a good Egoscue routine can help eliminate the need for ongoing acupuncturist and chiropractor visits.](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/)
**MELT (Myofascial Energetic Length **Technique)** is another powerful body work!**\[/FONT\] [https://www.meltmethod.com/](https://www.meltmethod.com/)

2 Re: _Just Breathe's_ home **Smart Meter**. Warren Woodard has fought for meter freedom for for health and privacy issues. [https://emfaction.userecho.com/communities/1/topics/115-smart-meters-create-nerve-blocking-frequencies-on-all-your-wires](https://emfaction.userecho.com/communities/1/topics/115-smart-meters-create-nerve-blocking-frequencies-on-all-your-wires) Sadly, I heard from his nephew that he recently lost his legal case. Many electric companies will allow folks to opt out based on health complaints. Might have to push some but possible and worth the effort. Replacing ours helped my headaches. Meter reader told me that Only 300 out of the whole area (48,000+) in Charlottesville, VA requested to opt out. Yikes! I have tried to get my HOA neighbors to consider it as it is easy and no cost to consumers here. We filled out a form and they promptly changed it back to a digital meter. At least they told me they did and the meter looks digital and says opt out on it. I'm not totally completely trusting of them.

3 Re: Just Breathe's mention that because of weight CPAP was considered but when I was unable to persuade my husband to try CPAP for his mild sleep apnea (known contributor of Afib) I was excited to find a simple solution that helped us both **How to Mouth Tape for Better Sleep** [https://askthedentist.com/mouth-tape-better-sleep/](https://askthedentist.com/mouth-tape-better-sleep/) He uses a paper tape and I use a blue colored very gentle wound tape by 3M found at Walmart or most any drugstore. Been using for about 3 years now. Eliminated his snoring and my mouth puffing and slight mouth breathing.
**Resulted in Immediate energy improvement for both of us! Easy and inexpensive.

I am here to learn and share so I hope my comments are helpful to someone.
Please let me know of any of decorum or site infractions.**

# Thread: Hello from Santa Barbara, CA!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-santa-barbara-ca.23993/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### April082 — 2019-07-26T23:23:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-santa-barbara-ca.23993/post-275041>

Been following/learning from Jack Kruse for almost a year now.
Born and raised Santa Barbara girl....have 3 young boys and a husband. In other words, I live in a frat house.
Feeling very fortunate to have a healthy happy family....now even more so thanks to adding sunrises, blue blockers, seafood & Malbec . Now I just need to move my family out of crazy California....I’ll miss the perfect weather but that’s about it! Would love to connect with other mitochondriacs.

### caroline — 2019-07-27T03:04:13+0300

There is a few families who have moved to Florida. Follow Liesel and others

Absolutely! My husband is a real estate broker out here so we have been in touch with several realtors in a few different states. CA is just too nutty. Between the 5G, homeless problem, vaccine mandates, and just too much damn government control over everything. No bueno!

### Dane Jacobsen — 2019-09-08T00:42:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-santa-barbara-ca.23993/post-277383>

> [April082 said:](/goto/post?id=275041)
>
> Been following/learning from Jack Kruse for almost a year now.
> Born and raised Santa Barbara girl....have 3 young boys and a husband. In other words, I live in a frat house.
> Feeling very fortunate to have a healthy happy family....now even more so thanks to adding sunrises, blue blockers, seafood & Malbec . Now I just need to move my family out of crazy California....I’ll miss the perfect weather but that’s about it! Would love to connect with other mitochondriacs.

I'm currently in Ojai but in route to the Big Island where I'll be working on an off-grid tropical nursery near the active Volcano. If you're in need of a good escape vehicle I'll be renting my Class C Gulfstream RV upon departure. It's been very beneficial for moving around and finding new places without having to plant roots too deep. Plus it forces you to embrace the beautiful discomfort of the outdoors.
Ojai is a bit better than Santa Barbara from the standpoint of population density but there's two gigantic cell towers, one of which is centrally located downtown. Goleta is loaded with technology, and an Airport, Santa Barbara is obviously not so bueno based on a few factors, and even the the Third district of SB county you have Vandenburg AFB, Lompoc, and the Airport bordering the Chumash Casino. I've tried them all but CA is imploding before our very eyes. Enjoy SB and know that there's a solid group of Mitochondriacs - in training - that practice Capoeira at Brasil Arts Cafe. Professor Daniel Chin Yoshimi and Jennifer Yanella Yoshimi - owners of BAC - would love to know that there's other folks that are learning from Dr. Jack. Bring the boys to Capoeira for Kids or jump into class with the adults. Introduce yourself to Chin as a Mitochondriac!
Best,
Dane

Thank you!!!!!!! I never knew that about BAC! I will have to bring my kids over there for sure. It’s nice to know I’m not the only Mito here in SB.
You are right...CA is going downhill fast. My kids see a wonderful naturopathic doctor in Ojai. I’ve always loved it there. Seems nowhere is safe anymore. We’ve been exploring the idea of moving to the Midwest, but who knows at this point.

### BrunoB — 2019-07-27T16:45:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paper-33-energy-from-food-any-have.23996/post-275062>

Hi good day! I saw this video.
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mOQ2SmaDLOY](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mOQ2SmaDLOY&fbclid=IwAR2DYmChgEtwqIH3p_ps1Y-CDYAOt_m3hCGe5Ksq4x9GsohJpgwQUpMArOg)
Does anyone have the paper that only 33% of the energy comes from food? Thanks in advance

### kris90 — 2019-07-31T22:37:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paper-33-energy-from-food-any-have.23996/post-275273>

I don't think there's a specific paper stating it. It's probably based on calculations. I think it has a lot to do with Gilbert Ling's work on energy generated from water splitting and ATP recycling. I remember reading we make our own bodyweight in ATP in a day, so it cannot be possible that food is behind those requirements.

### maria@modernhuman — 2024-10-03T05:58:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paper-33-energy-from-food-any-have.23996/post-340351>

aw man I was looking for the calculation too. and of course the video link where the 33% is mentioned is no longer available as that account "has been terminated" by youtube. I saw Jack mention in another post that the whole ATP comes from food notion breaks the 2nd law of thermodynamics by 500%\*(he says in one post) and 500 times\*(in another post) but there was no calculation. if anyone sees any equations or calc methodologies feel free to share. I understand the high level concept that we're not eating over our body weight in pure atp molecules daily but still I like seeing calc methodology if it's available.

this blog is a good reference point: [https://jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-10-quantum-puzzle/](https://jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-10-quantum-puzzle/)

and here is a comment post that is also helpful: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/post-216294](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/post-216294)

# Thread: Menstrual flow

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LieselK — 2019-07-29T04:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menstrual-flow.23997/post-275090>

Just last month and this month I noticed that my menstrual flow is super thin- I use menstrual cups so I can actually tell. Interestingly just last month is when I finally, overall, started to feel "great" again (like I felt last summer)! If a thicker flow is due to excess estrogen causing build-up in the uterus lining then it follows that my thin flow means my estrogen is down? I don't get cramps however I did notice an increase in blood flow to my pelvic floor on Day 1 this time as has been the case in the past.

Anyway, yay for sunshine!

# Thread: Ajohnson90s Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90s-optimal-journal.24001/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ajohnson90 — 2019-07-30T00:34:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90s-optimal-journal.24001/post-275146>

Hello fellow Mitochondriacs

I’m posting here to chronicle my journey into dis-ease as well as hopefully gather some insight on how I might come out the other side healthier and happier

I’m going to list my current state of health, what issues are bothering me most, and then work my way backwards chronologically in terms of symptoms.

Currently- I’ve been living with intense brain fog for the last three years. I literally feel like I’m in a fog 24/7. Critical thinking, pattern recognition (like chess for example) cause and effect and abstract thinking are not impossible but almost. Words can be difficult to find, I often lose my thoughts mid sentence and concentrating is quite the task. I often find myself stuck on the same page reading, and even when I’m at my most cognizant, the information doesn’t feel like it’s registering.

If situations get too stressful (my bar for stress has lowered to “I’m at the grocery store and there’s too many people and I’m not cognizant enough to even put together a grocery list let alone bring it”) I dissociate and feel high or drunk.

I feel like if I could just take a blast of cold water to the face it would all go away. But it doesn’t.

This has been accompanied by anxiety, depression, mood swings, fatigue, tight muscles, heart palpitations, panic attacks, tremors during sleep, dissociative feelings, a temporary loss of focus in vision, floaters in eyes and I often shake whenever I try to create tension in my body.

Simply bracing my abs causes me to shake like I just worked out for hours.

The brain fog started in May 2016 and after some stressful events I started experiencing the tremors, dissociation, heart palpitations, pins and needles in my limbs and fatigue around November 2016.

They let up for a few months in 2017 until my whole world gave out from underneath of me when I lost my dad, my new girlfriend turned out to be abusive, I lost my job and my apartment all in a about five months. The symptoms have persisted since then.

In 2015 I started experiencing some trouble sleeping. It was summer and I started having night sweats, sleeping for 10-14 hours a day, and generally having trouble staying or feeling hydrated (constant dry mouth, yellow urine in the morning, which was new for me). I assumed it was stress from the heat, my intense workout regimen, my new job, and my marriage falling apart.

Months go by, the weather cools off and my symptoms persist.

I would wake up feeling groggy, like I had been drugged or just hungover.

My coffee consumption also increased in this time (my intuition tells me this is one of the primary causes) and I can have some pretty adverse reactions to too much caffeine- all symptomatic of hypoglycemia and orthostatic hypotension.

Between 2010 and 2015 the only symptoms I had were random bouts of hypoglycemia- which would usually happen on road trips(?), and my wisdom teeth creating tension in my job, disrupting my sleep.

In 2010 I had similar symptoms to the ones I have now- fatigue, orthostatic hypotension, feeling hypoglycemic, etc. it passed after a few months. This was shortly after switching to a vegetarian diet, which I maintained until 2015. After consuming animal products my health greatly improved.

Currently I still have my four wisdom teeth (awaiting extraction and contemplating the possibility of any tension within the mandible and cervical spine causing the brain fog issue). I grind my teeth all the time because of it- thinking that act alone could be disrupting my sleep, causing the issues, and keeping me in a elevated stress response.

I also have 8 silver fillings, one of which is cracked. I’m not sure how long it’s been cracked, but am contemplating that could be causing the issue.

I’m really making an effort to reduce all nnEMF and restore my circadian rhythm, but not seeing much of an improvement.

I’m at the point where I’m feeling very hopeless about this situation.

I have had some basic panels done- sedimentation rate, vitamin d 1, 25 dihydroxy, basics metabolic panel, TSH w/reflecx to T4, and CBC. Nothing stands out to my doctor to warrant further testing, he thinks it’s “stress”.

I’ve been tested for sleep apnea, which turned out negative.

I’ve also had an ecg and ctc done- nothing abnormal either.

I can post any specific levels upon request.

I’d like to test for metals, allergens, parasites, fungi, etc- I just need to find a good doctor and save up the money to afford the tests.

If you guys have any info or insight I will greatly appreciate it. I prefer hints toward solving the puzzle myself, so as not burden others for doing my own work to answer these questions. But I’m not always certain what questions need asking.

I just want to fix my brain so I can live fully in the present

Thanks!

### caroline — 2019-07-31T06:47:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90s-optimal-journal.24001/post-275247>

where do you live? work?

what do you eat? seafood?

drink? what kind of water

give Dr. K. your Mom's and Gran's health history.

welcome

### caroline — 2019-07-31T06:48:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90s-optimal-journal.24001/post-275248>

Do you listen to Jack's podcasts? His Nourish Vermont talks?

Patreon member? Klub member?

you are going to need lots of skin in the game!

do you see every sunrise? sunset?

The three legged stool .....light, water and magnetism

### ajohnson90 — 2020-02-04T23:22:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90s-optimal-journal.24001/post-285626>

In September 2019, I had my wisdom teeth removed and noticed a marked improvement in sleep and thus my quality of life. Instead of waking up groggy, and having yellow, frothy urine, I woke up well-rested with clear urine. Even the quality of my erections improved.
That only lasted a month, and I have slowly slid back to poor sleep. I started grinding my teeth again and I haven't slept well since. My brain fog never improved, but I felt that if my sleep had continued improving I might have seen some change in my cognitive function.
It was nice to feel better for once and I'm hopeful I can start to feel that way again.

Here are some test results that my neurologist ran. It would be nice to get an quantum biology assessment of them.
She has put me on beta-blockers as an antagonist for adrenaline to calm my SNS. I really don't want to take them. I don't believe it's the root of the issue.

My vitamin B levels haven't ever been low, and I've never supplemented with it, but seeing these results, I've picked up some desiccated liver tablets to supplement my diet.

I quit drinking caffeine and my anxiety and heart palpitations have gone away. Alcohol is next on the list to cut out.

I just want to be present, engaged and actually remember things.

EDIT: About a month before I got my wisdom teeth removed, I was taking magnesium l-threonate and CoQ10. I didn't notice any improvement in my daily life until after I got the teeth removed, but I am currently taking Mag L-Threonate and CoQ10 again to see if it will recreate the same impact.

I'm also wondering if the antibiotics my dentist gave me could have played a role in the change in my sleep as well. I've since taken a different kind of antibiotics for epididymitis but noticed no changes other than the prescribed issue.

### ajohnson90 — 2020-02-04T23:30:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90s-optimal-journal.24001/post-285628>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=275247)
>
> where do you live? work?
>
> what do you eat? seafood?
>
> drink? what kind of water
>
> give Dr. K. your Mom's and Gran's health history.
>
> welcome

Hi Caroline, I apologize for the late reply.

I live in Oklahoma City, OK, USA.

My diet is varied. Time restricted with the sun. I eat pretty "clean", lot's of veggies, healthy starches and proteins with a lot of fat. I do eat plenty of seafood, though I've noticed that since I've stopped eating as much seafood recently my skin has cleared up.

Water is unfortunately tap water. Working on building my income to get access to better water, if not just a Berkey filter.

I do not see every single sunrise or sunset, though I try, and I'm working on building my life so I can see it. Hence, I've just left my place of work and moved near a park to have access to a green space. I wear blue blockers after the sun goes down and am working to minimize nEMF exposure (no wifi, phone on airplane mode)

I'm not a Patreon or Klub member, but have devoured all of the free material on YT, Jack's site and podcasts.

My grandmother is 65, very overweight and has a pacemaker. No other apparent issues. Apparently cardiac issues "run in the family", but she eats SAD, is physically inactive and doesn't go outside.
My mother had a benign mass in her upper back removed about a decade ago, gall bladder removed, severe mental health issues, overweight, arthritis she is a former drug user, eats a SAD, smokes cigarettes, inactive, indoors, etc.

### caroline — 2020-02-05T02:21:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90s-optimal-journal.24001/post-285634>

Baby steps .....skin in the game

### ajohnson90 — 2023-10-30T20:00:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90s-optimal-journal.24001/post-327680>

So I just wanted to reach out and update you all on an experience I had and get some advice.
After a sleep study in April 2021, I was diagnosed with mild obstructive sleep apnea, with an AHI of 2. I tried a mandibular repositioning device and CPAP. Neither of which worked, I had a follow up sleep study to adjust the CPAP and it made things worse, I would use the CPAP just fine for a week or three and then I would have trouble falling asleep because I'd start choking as soon as I started dozing off. This got worse after they adjusted it.

During this time I moved to a more northern latitude, a higher altitude and a drier climate (SLC, UT) and my health went on a rapid decline. All sorts of new issues popped up and my existing symptoms worsened. I struggled to walk up a single flight of stairs, panic attacks left me bedridden. Paranoia and depression ensued. It was awful.

Fast foward to April 2022 and I am taking Erwan Le Corre's BreathHoldWork Meditation Course. Having studied and practiced breathwork for a long time, I knew if I could get my breath hold to 5 minutes I'd improve a lot of the symptoms I was experience- particularly the anxiety and panic attacks. The work consists of holding your breath and meditating to create the real, lived experience that you want- say contentment or relaxation- during intense neurophysiological stress of breath holding. So lots of practice in regards to relaxation, body scanning, perception, etc. As well as traditional breathhold work tables. One of the exercises involves tensing your soft palate to allow the diaphragm to relax and the air to move back up into the head, but since the soft palate is contracted, the air doesn't leave the body. I started the course at a :30 breathhold, and after 4 weeks I had a 1:45, repeatable breathhold. Not very impressive but a huge jump and a big increase in awareness, reaction, and understanding.

On the last day of the course I made an attempt at a max breathhold and it was less than my previous attempt and I was bummed about this but after the course was over that day, I was walking to a martial arts class and felt a relief come over me- like I'd had a pair of vice grips around me the last decade or so. The tension in my jaw let go and my breath softened and I felt clarity of mind. I hadn't felt that relaxed in over 7 years.
That night I slept like a baby and woke up feeling refreshed for the first time in 8 years. Every day after continued in the way, feeling better, and sleeping better, and like a feedback loop, my life just improved. My sleep, my digestion, my cognitive function, my mental health, my strength, it all came back and I felt amazing. I started working out again and it felt good, instead of liking beating myself up. It was no longer difficult to walk up the stairs, I didn't get winded or light headed or anything. Because I felt good, I could feel tangible effect of seeing the sun rise and set and getting in cold water and eating well. Before it felt like it made no difference.

I don't know what happened but this only last 4-5 months and my sleep went back to the way it was before and my health fell apart. I've tried doing the breathwork practices but they don't offer the same relief or results.

I need to figure out what's going on, why this happened, and how to fix it so I can be get my life back.
Any insight, any questions or feedback are deeply appreciated.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

are a class of medications that are used to treat various conditions such as high blood pressure,

that maybe indication that your cardiovascular system is rigid, and possibly full of crud (possibly heart attack may be nearby).

they are not ciprofloxacin type, there is few of them

While at ciprofloxacin,
you are likely brushing your teeth with toothpaste (that has fluoride).

the other
it kills bacteria in your mouth, making sure that you will not be able to make Nitric Oxide (mentioned by me above).
\===========================================
**Epididymitis** is a medical condition characterized by inflammation of the epididymis, a tube located at the back of the testicle that carries sperm.
It can be caused by a bacterial infection, including sexually transmitted infections such as gonorrhea or chlamydia, urinary tract infections, or prostate infections.
Symptoms of epididymitis include:

Swollen scrotum
Painful urination
Blood in semen
Pain in the testicle
Discharge from the penis
Pain during sexual intercourse
Groin pain and pressure in the testicles
Tenderness on the testicles
Redness and warmth in the scrotum
\------------

You better get
DUTCHtest.com

Your steroid hormones maybe screwed.
Also get,
Coronary calcium Scan. The number is called the Agatston score.
Insurance does not pay for that, but it cost $100.

\=====================================

# Thread: Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal.24002/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 91

### wildperoxin — 2019-07-30T01:45:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal.24002/post-275150>

As I'm working my way up to journal here, I was unsure whether to start my Optimal Journal with a backlog or begin with where I am. I'll begin with where I am, the present, and add a backlog as appropriate.

BACKGROUND: Currently, I live with family. I have a water distiller which I cannot find all the parts too, so I drink filtered spring/mineral water from bottled sources or via a filter; best in quality available. I see the sun in the AM, and spend as much time in the garden when at home as possible. I have a Kiniki poncho and wear this while out (so as not to startle the neighbours). At times I wear a silk dressing gown, inside and outside, and suede-soled shoes.

Typical AM, skipping the details: workout routine in the garden, food, cold bath.

In the evenings: red lights and candles in doors. Blue light blocking glasses. I want to get some that are clear for work so I can pass them off as reading glasses and for focus (I have astigmatism).

We have LED colour changing bulbs around the house. Chromotherapy is very interesting. I have blackout curtains in my room; I need to fix the fitting soon. It is very helpful for now nevertheless.

The main aim over the past year has been Ethernet housewide: ethernet switches, and an EMF metre to boot. Try that with teenagers in the house who have known nothing but wifi and smart phones all of their lives. I suggested to my mother that she buys a safe, and they put there phones in there at 7pm, 9pm latest (suggesting they don't have smart phones at all sounds extreme to them). There are a bunch of useful tools and resources that have been locked away for years because of a broken door. It is due to be fixed on August 29th, and I hope it does. 5G has rolled out in the city family and I live in. Spending a small fortune for tools, necessary tools, where and when money could be used elsewhere or elsewise.

As mentioned previously, going back to my home country 2° south of the equator saved my life. When I turned 18 I got access to money from compensation and paid to for tickets for my family to go back.

In between then and now, it's been a rough ride with mental health, addictions, academia, and and and - it's been life, through a glass darkly. What I can only explain as getting memory gaps/holes, among other things. In late 2017, I decided to take a break from university and seek out biofeedback, of which I had been aware of for years - and my intuition has been pointing me to since 2013 or so. Best decision I made in 2017. Life became real, or more real. Throughout my teenages years I was incapable of retaining information well. I could understand and grasp concepts, but the magnitude and import combined with memory gaps made it hard to integrate.

CT RX: I aim for 160 minutes plus weekly of cold baths with a minimum of 15 minutes weekly of head immersions while during the cold baths. I might throw in cold showers, it depends. I enjoy these cold baths greatly.

On a related note: I read Epi Paleo Rx years ago (between 2013 and 2015: I can check, it took me a while to read because... a) long list of books to read; b) when it comes to some aspects of topics such as these, hearing it - or both hearing it and reading it - are preferred. Audiobook anytime soon?).

I did the Leptin Rx, or a leptin reset diet back in 2013. Overall, I have more questions now than I have answers, so bear with me...

WRT diet: I like seafood, seafood likes me. Eating locally and seasonally, low carb paleo, animal-based. I like pork too. In my native country, as in the country I live in, they are found a plenty. This is good news.

I'm trying to find more information on hormones and psychological health. In the past perhaps my need to know why got in way of how. Now I just want to learn more bushcraft and everything will be alright

I'll continue with this journal soon. 'Till then, best!

### wildperoxin — 2019-07-30T16:44:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal.24002/post-275178>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=275155)
>
> [https://jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/](https://jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/)

The only (labs) testing I have done is genetic, via 23andme. Getting a full hormone, O6:O3 and mineral panel is something I will definitely be doing in future, at least once in my life. Now I feel that future is nearer than later; sooner is better. Meanwhile sorting the finances.

What I noticed with Omega 3 is mood improvement, as if serotonin registers; it depends on diet and environmental factors also. With animal based epi-paleo, raw or cooked, my mood seems okay without them relatively speaking (on days I miss supplementing). I take 3g-5g, depending. We live near the coast so local seafood is available. I think cost is the main issue. I've turned to tinned and frozen when getting fresh from the market was not viable.

I'd like a hormone and O6:O3 panel asap for various reasons.

Looking back over the protocols, I've done some components maybe of each, and would like to revisit to execute them better, if need be. Also considering I did them without taking labs, so the data was more qualitative than quantitative. For example, I did the Adrenal Fx and Leaky Gut Fx when I lived on my own before university. Cold thermogenesis in the form of cold baths I started did back then also. The university environment changed a lot of things.

Pregnenolene and cortisol: I have high anxiety levels (excitatory-inhibitory responses) and undiagnosed neurodevelopmental muscoskeletal or spatial/coordination factors... the environment is a big factor also. Might doing a protocol with pregnenolene be an option? Alongside testing?

There is a lot of work ahead. I'm happy at the stage I am, knowing the nature of the journey, reality and the discoveries unearthed along the way. To have my mother turn the red lights on at night and see she has taken in some of what we discuss, is a blessing. She has high blood pressure. I got a blood pressure monitor. When she asked for my advice I said get rid of the medication, get out in the AM barefoot or use UVA/IR lighting, and eat more seafood and organ meats. It crossed my mind that iodine and magnesium may help her also (supplements are not necessary a go to and should be considered contextually).

I'm so happy without coffee, I wonder why I didn't stop before. I could say the ssme for alcohol - hence addictions.

### wildperoxin — 2019-08-01T10:06:29+0300

Can you cite a good reference/study/read/source?

It's worth finding references and looking into. Let's do a little digging.

What comes to mind: it might be to do with the body's way of protecting degenerative areas of proteins and cells (brain - heart -nervous system - myelin sheath) from states of energy deficiencies/depletion and apoptosis. The EPA and DHA may be found in higher concentrations in certain areas, and not others, and are not able to act effectively in any case to alter or may even promote the course neolithic disease progression.

EPA might be higher than DHA. Peroxisomes may not be able to break down certain fatty acid chains, on either a genetic, congenital or acquired basis. In turn this affects signaling, voltage and current across and in cells, and communications in the systems. The ability for EZ zones to be formed or sustained extra-/intra-cellularly is affected. There's something up with the water the body's able to make, so the water (and fats!) they take in matter more.

So what was once an adaptive response, a temporary solution, becomes a disease.

In terms of medical imaging, I vaguely recall their nervous systems and brain look different (I forget which one - more white areas than grey/dark).

It might be O6 that's dysregulated, and seafood helps there. Plus good water.

This is all subject to questioning, and I'm speculatin' or thinking out loud, so maybe read this as questions, dots-connections in complex systems or points of inquiry/reference. At some point today, I'll check out what data or evidence can tell us.

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/), all your posts make me stop and think!
